And we must also remember what is possible when we all work together and keep the faith in our democracy. Happy Memorial Day, Kristen | This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. But if you would like to . | Paid for by Kristen for Michigan Kristen for Michigan P.O. Box 854 Bay City, MI 48707 United States

name: Michael B. Moore for South Carolina
subject: "On Message" From Michael B. Moore
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
On Message
from Michael B. Moore
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
Welcome to our campaign newsletter.
We're happy you're here.
We're using this space to keep you updated on all the latest stories, events, and opportunities related to Michael's run for South Carolina's 1st Congressional District.
Stay tuned — we'll be in your inbox every Monday.
See you on the trail.
Join our movement [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
On the calendar
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104585/images/IMG_8565.jpg]
On this Memorial Day, we honor and mourn those brave U.S. military personnel who died defending our fundamental freedoms.
We are forever indebted to the fallen service members from South Carolina and across the nation who made the ultimate sacrifice for their fellow Americans.
In the news
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
Our campaign just earned the endorsement of the nation's largest federal employee union, the American Federation of Government Employees [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress/posts/pfbid0wfjVeju8ewyRN5r2nAznEFGUzaUyPSFLLGLZsBHq5gZxLprSLpsSQ3EqESUA1heMl] !
AFGE r epresents 750,000 workers spread across 900 local unions, including nurses, park rangers, TSA agents, and more. Michael stands with all of them — just like he stands with the working families of SC-01.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
Kenneth Canty [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress/posts/pfbid034nKxyxkQm9aPJ59chbUnDwYyvEkPRTnX4VaqoTwdomWsHLL2QGtmWyMdW5yegaBml] — who has worked on some of the biggest infrastructure projects along the Eastern Seaboard — also announced his support for our campaign!
Kenneth and Michael agree on the importance of investing in t he Lowcountry's roads and bridges, and Michael is grateful to have earned his endorsement in the race for SC-01.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
In other news, the Summerville Journal Scene [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress/posts/pfbid02Vod2YSKbJFCFL2CykiwAfMzKJrr7fQW23hrfpwqgcvMoMzCKrHa9ULeqbtzkPvvCl] covered our campaign for Congress — including Michael's plan to energize Lowcountry Democrats, persuade independent-minded Republicans, and assemble the coalition needed to win in November.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
Michael also agreed to participate in a Democratic primary debate hosted by WCBD [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress/posts/pfbid02JJEqfAmwsXjzwtxZgGN31BpnXhsY1Qca3fyTBscqb3KEfdhVxL9Nf1oWXE87KVGyl] . He's excited to dive into the issues that matter most to the working families of SC-01!
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
Finally, Michael weighed in on the Supreme Court's ruling [https://x.com/michael_b_moore/status/1793695556046606417] in the SC-01 racial gerrymandering case. We have a plan to win in November, no matter the playing field, and we're more confident than ever about our footing in the fight for this swing district.
From the trail
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
Thanks to Judy Hines for hosting our campaign at her home in Charleston's Bishop Gadsden Episcopal Retirement Community [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress/posts/pfbid02iMHiAp7w2NbPTMvjCbFDy2KxcuHk1Ph4qFe9RH7psUJtMssns1T8iG6KemreUjXbl] !
In Congress, Michael will fight to stop extreme Republicans from raising the retirement age, slashing your benefits, and cutting Social Security and Medicare.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
The James Island-Folly Beach Democrats [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress/posts/pfbid0Ue2E4D3G53Hy9U4h9GnkjCa2BnkpEweFtPXtdrFEVErCwiMeJpJ2k3sabVncpqYKl] had a terrific turnout at their candidate forum.
Sheriff Kristin Graziano's closing comments were spot on: Every single one of us will need to show up to help flip SC-01 in November!
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
Michael participated in the latest installment of the Post and Courier's "Pints & Politics" series [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress/posts/pfbid0oJd3EVNEqa6LSwT8booBW4gmfRcmdx5EqR5NZgUvJHrDWN2KTaNaYUtvqffJsDkNl] , sponsored by AARP South Carolina.
From safeguarding reproductive freedom to fostering a more inclusive economy, he's ready to tackle the issues facing working families in SC-01.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
Our team had a great time over the weekend at the Original Gullah Festival [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress/posts/pfbid0xXrrvXgawM4MEvmwfQZHLjFTmeh6L8dSaowf79XHDp4cz1kS7UH8WC2NF1RNehmyl] in Beaufort!
Thanks to the organizers for allowing Michael to share the stage with so many beloved leaders from our community. We enjoyed meeting the talented vendors — and tasting so much amazing Gullah cuisine!
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
Michael shared his hopeful vision for the future of SC-01 at the May meeting of the Goose Creek NAACP [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress/posts/pfbid032SuaJessaqQF67qash9Mg38YFttiWLufUXQVqdbyxzzy8EFqBFGZbVCPSjE6fs2Bl] .
As a child, Michael's grandfather gave him the special gift of a lifetime membership to the NAACP — and Michael recently got to do the same for his 10-month-old granddaughter, Sasha!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104585/images/IMG_8562.jpg]
On Sunday afternoon, Michael met with supporters on Johns Island [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress/posts/pfbid0qamNzUxSKhehnm433wQAR4VLGAe2ud5R6dA4QD8HYKyt2Y6KXGMwT37vvQynYS55l] , where they discussed some of the most pressing problems for this coastal community.
Thanks to Elizabeth Coaxum for hosting our campaign in your home, and to former Charleston County Council Member Anna Johnson for helping organize this event!
On our team
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter]
There's so much work to do, and we're only just getting started.
Join us in our fight to flip this swing district.
Together, we're going to build a brighter future for South Carolina.
Donate [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/michael.b.moore-sc.1st-newsletter] Volunteer [https://www.michaelbmoore.com/volunteer]
https://twitter.com/michael_b_moore [https://twitter.com/michael_b_moore] https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress [https://www.facebook.com/MichaelBMooreforCongress] https://instagram.com/https://www.instagram.com/mbm_sc01/ [https://instagram.com/https://www.instagram.com/mbm_sc01/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/moore4sc/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/moore4sc/] https://www.tiktok.com/@michaelbmoore4sc [https://www.tiktok.com/@michaelbmoore4sc]
Copyright (C) Michael B. Moore for South Carolina. All rights reserved.
Michael B. Moore for South Carolina
3022 South Morgans Point Road Suite 295
Mount Pleasant, SC 29466
United States

name: Chris Deluzio
subject: Honoring the fallen
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRIS DELUZIO FOR CONGRESS
body:
Derek,

I know that today many people may gather with family and friends for a BBQ, attend a local parade, or head out of town for a weekend away. But I hope that for a moment, maybe even while you read this email, you pause and remember what this day is all about.

Today, on Memorial Day, we reflect on the Americans who gave this country the last full measure of devotion, who laid down their lives for the promise of America.

Many Western PA families have experienced the tragedy of losing a loved one who gave this country their all. And I know that today may be an emotional day for many of you.

So for those who carry the pain from someone in their lives who paid the ultimate price: Their sacrifice and their memory will never be forgotten.

Thank you for taking the time to honor the fallen on this Memorial Day,

Chris Deluzio
Make sure this email goes to your inbox. Add info@chrisforpa.com to your address book. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. But if you would like to unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/z5DxLUzOH0Cbr-Ed1epOKA2?unsubscribedata=FkG9oBWHCr7X2MvGAWag7pSqX4QTdKn6lIDqepee2haR7FxZa0V5zByDnoRisOCknE3%2FDmMRwTqZS%2Bp6dT6VQk%2FK4OIw176n3KUooBj%2FwPbIsr5IXyK9Leu2dtoQY5D8yztypJAEdYwjVbSYxu5t%2FdRsez2NAf4%2BrKf4N7hg8enzPQvX%2FpKNAmMWlh0xW1OJlQAFSH24En6w6Us4nuPJZbilz1PJHkBynN7%2F5HWzemU19y4bQJwm9sT9eINtP3v4LIcb%2BlZX%2FubIU3GZFLwlMqOgZ96PuE%2FPKKq3IvUOf0I7j4%2Bi3LF6vSUmhm7odBXFNfYuu92h5rHN%2BbfM8FFASLRFXISkstg%2BsitnfmrCyS3Gb9Y899BIiDxpDrtos2DyXYaUoywknIx58qrpXo7MP%2BOYYxJTOBYXSN90Y%2B0dzKlt%2BlDObA7D6ZgCpXmVfeM5 .

name: Russell Prescott
subject: Remember and Honor
body:
| | |
---
| [](https://sxw89cgbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001DL3H1dQgsKe0VIYfgy4bKhNbkPdqzNTypFdYAj7j_2-Bz_xR1q1n3BSHh2dq3RXeP-dWT17Fmt6k0O3Zcf6NXaijqwxYqQg2pSdRcCmdo_UsmNcY__KJ-_otUbmsBwn_vDbZofdMc0gNwHSz4Qrv84nfW8yORDtp2i7x5NcrVfI=&c=3abGtqXAaf_WJE0u-g5KPDv5oB7L2weXcieN1OIi3Hzjd5ZmXyTgbw==&ch=vNKhus_OBVo8suWNrv-IrAqgyFga3kdlH18VyzDirtOaR_WeRUaj_Q==)
---
| Peter,
Today, we honor and remember the brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our country.
Their courage and selflessness are a testament to the ideals of freedom and democracy that we hold dear.
---
| [](https://sxw89cgbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001DL3H1dQgsKe0VIYfgy4bKhNbkPdqzNTypFdYAj7j_2-Bz_xR1q1n3BSHh2dq3RXeP-dWT17Fmt6k0O3Zcf6NXaijqwxYqQg2pSdRcCmdo_UsmNcY__KJ-_otUbmsBwn_vDbZofdMc0gNwHSz4Qrv84nfW8yORDtp2i7x5NcrVfI=&c=3abGtqXAaf_WJE0u-g5KPDv5oB7L2weXcieN1OIi3Hzjd5ZmXyTgbw==&ch=vNKhus_OBVo8suWNrv-IrAqgyFga3kdlH18VyzDirtOaR_WeRUaj_Q==)
---
| On this Memorial Day, let us pause to pay tribute to those who gave their lives to protect our nation and its core values. Their sacrifice will never be forgotten.
As we spend time with loved ones today, let us also take a moment to express our gratitude to the veterans and active-duty service members who continue to protect our freedom every day.
May we always remember the true meaning of Memorial Day and the debt of gratitude we owe to those who have served and sacrificed for us.
God Bless America,
Russell Prescott
---
---
| [](https://sxw89cgbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001DL3H1dQgsKe0VIYfgy4bKhNbkPdqzNTypFdYAj7j_2-Bz_xR1q1n3BSHh2dq3RXeP-dWT17Fmt6k0O3Zcf6NXaijqwxYqQg2pSdRcCmdo_UsmNcY__KJ-_otUbmsBwn_vDbZofdMc0gNwHSz4Qrv84nfW8yORDtp2i7x5NcrVfI=&c=3abGtqXAaf_WJE0u-g5KPDv5oB7L2weXcieN1OIi3Hzjd5ZmXyTgbw==&ch=vNKhus_OBVo8suWNrv-IrAqgyFga3kdlH18VyzDirtOaR_WeRUaj_Q==)

---
| [PrescottforCongress.com](https://sxw89cgbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001DL3H1dQgsKe0VIYfgy4bKhNbkPdqzNTypFdYAj7j_2-Bz_xR1q1n3Fi-njRohhv0wpxt3Eyd7gp8Edlo9A9A1zWO6cJ8yrILWKLtFA4Vuce3IcVm9KQ9p7DjZPlvrCwWlUmxnydPoijt_-bXntfdtpeTWqygWQxH&c=3abGtqXAaf_WJE0u-g5KPDv5oB7L2weXcieN1OIi3Hzjd5ZmXyTgbw==&ch=vNKhus_OBVo8suWNrv-IrAqgyFga3kdlH18VyzDirtOaR_WeRUaj_Q==)
---
| A note from Russell...
The people of New Hampshire are ready for a change in our representation in Congress, whether it's rising energy costs, the broken southern border, or a lack of support for our men and women in law enforcement. New Hampshire families know that our current leadership in Washington is failing us and have forgotten the 'Live Free or Die' values that we all pride ourselves on in New Hampshire. We need a fresh voice who has the business and elected experience to change the status quo. United, we can defeat Chris Pappas and bring real change to Washington.
Thank you.
-Russell

clean_body: | | | | == | Peter, Today, we honor and remember the brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our country. Their courage and selflessness are a testament to the ideals of freedom and democracy that we hold dear. | == | On this Memorial Day, let us pause to pay tribute to those who gave their lives to protect our nation and its core values. Their sacrifice will never be forgotten. As we spend time with loved ones today, let us also take a moment to express our gratitude to the veterans and active-duty service members who continue to protect our freedom every day. May we always remember the true meaning of Memorial Day and the debt of gratitude we owe to those who have served and sacrificed for us. God Bless America, Russell Prescott | == | Follow Russell on Social Media: | == == == | == | A note from Russell... The people of New Hampshire are ready for a change in our representation in Congress, whether it's rising energy costs, the broken southern border, or a lack of support for our men and women in law enforcement. New Hampshire families know that our current leadership in Washington is failing us and have forgotten the 'Live Free or Die' values that we all pride ourselves on in New Hampshire. We need a fresh voice who has the business and elected experience to change the status quo. United, we can defeat Chris Pappas and bring real change to Washington. Thank you. -Russell Peter \- Forward this message to 5 FRIENDS AND FAMILY MEMBERS and help us raise awareness for our mission to achieve a better future for New Hampshire. name: Tim Burchett
subject: Honoring Uncle Roy’s Sacrifice 🪖
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Burchett for Congress**
body:
Why Memorial Day is personal for me...

| | | |
---
| |
---
| |
---
Friend, Memorial Day is deeply personal for me as it’s an opportunity to remember and honor my Uncle Roy, a man who made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our nation. Uncle Roy didn’t have to go to war—he was a college graduate with his whole life ahead of him. But my mama’s brother, the oldest of seven kids, felt a profound duty that inspired him to enlist in the Army. He served during World War II and fell in Normandy just two weeks after the D-Day invasion, never having the chance to get married or start a family of his own. Instead, he gave his life for the freedoms we cherish in America—freedoms that most people around the world can only imagine. Growing up, I always noticed a tear in my mama’s eye whenever she heard the national anthem, and I know she was thinking of her big brother and the countless others who willingly laid down their lives for our country. May their memories inspire us to show gratitude for those who made America what it is today and to protect the values they fought for. Sincerely, Tim Burchett
---

I couldn’t help myself — I checked Adam’s ActBlue page this morning to see how we were pacing toward our end-of-month fundraising goal.
Bad news: our fundraising slowed _way down_ over the weekend.

name: Juan Ciscomani
subject: Memorial Day 2024
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CISCOMANI FOR CONGRESS
body:
|
---
| | [ ](https://juanciscomani.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ed32a393c9fe1089f10fee94&id=008baff242&e=659fba240e)
---
| | In honor of Memorial Day, I want to take a moment to remember the brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice while serving our country and defending our freedom. Their selflessness and courage will never be forgotten, and we owe them a debt of gratitude – one that can never be fully repaid.
As we gather with friends and family over the long weekend, let’s take time to remember the true meaning of Memorial Day and the sacrifices made by our servicemembers and their families.
Throughout my campaign, I've had the privilege of working closely with surviving loved ones of the fallen, veterans, military personnel, and their families. I am dedicated to advocating for them and their loved ones. From fighting for better healthcare and benefits to ensuring they receive the support they need, I've been proud to stand alongside those who have served.
**Our fight here in AZ-06 is far from over. We must stand together to defend this seat and ensure your voice is heard in Congress.**
* **[Volunteer](https://juanciscomani.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ed32a393c9fe1089f10fee94&id=d7e354ef99&e=659fba240e):** Join Team Ciscomani to spread the word about our campaign. With less than 45 days until ballots drop, we need everyone!
* **[Share](https://juanciscomani.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4ed32a393c9fe1089f10fee94&id=e414d85faf&e=659fba240e): **** ** Spread the word! Share our emails with friends, invite them to an upcoming event, and tell your neighbors about our campaign.

Thank you to all who have served, to those who continue to serve, and to the families of fallen heroes. Your dedication and bravery inspire us all. Juan

| | Today’s a sacred day, as you know. So as we observe Memorial Day, it’s important to reflect on the sacrifices our fallen military heroes have made throughout history. The safety, opportunities, and freedoms we enjoy are only possible because men and women in the U.S. Armed Forces have been willing to risk their lives fighting our country’s enemies. We must never forget the price they paid for us. God bless,
Mike Sapraicone

body:

| | , On Memorial Day, Americans honor those who gave “the last full measure of devotion” to our country. I hope you enjoy some down time as we reflect on the sacrifices American servicemembers made for us. It’s truly a blessing to be an American, and we can never forget the fallen soldiers, sailors, airmen, Coast Guardsmen and Marines throughout history who gave everything to keep us free. God bless,
Brad Finstad

body:
A few years ago, members of the world’s elite met at the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, and launched the Great Reset initiative.

| | | | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_s=0d74c95fbd4d4102a7d20a9b22700207&_t=8J9viTF7JjEBoUToPZlpTt04JFeMawFcR6Z6gh_zbY8V50NTSO-usd61rpmdDZlBzIrw6ROED3BWoxYy0g_omTF-ILB7HOv9nqrkIUw_HAo%3D)_

| | [](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=0d74c95fbd4d4102a7d20a9b22700207&_e=3gCfrM7zOHSnRdr8Q4gIWJ-ae-p666yTUA0wM4yfHvGhlqt4R0Kf1V0GXQe2LUn88KZ03WGnsOThxJGVtW7txTwBk7NIPmblWgSVkToY3SfvRBgsBbWnrLEX-yREw6XTxdayNuqdulOYkeL4mxVBmUUZE8FleYTZeiLyOBPkWh-NHoFGy8Lj6F9ognCgmy_JafTYbwc3Kd0WIS4yUqF-fj9XsGMA-fZAQvnyMolhDuJ_zB8VQOqwstSj01ejM1O9Da6TyT8DEAZYYc7GSYs_YRzKwRbruHHogTXgX6iwII8vkBsXkIJH2yflm15RcOF-M8eZQZtIvmGGsR0Xwl_dN54tg5IUgy-Wdke1yqQHRflriWORvytZ6Ka6MoZ0Lza8BRbYKjJf9pY6pELKDqr7Rw%3D%3D) Dear Fellow American,

A few years ago, members of the world's elite met at the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, and launched the Great Reset initiative.

What is their plan, and what does it mean for America?

In short, they aim to transform the world economy. As Klaus Schwab--the founder and executive director of the Forum--wrote, "We need a 'Great Reset' of capitalism." If successful, their plan would also have the effect of destroying our American way of life.

This initiative is not merely about economic reform. It is an attempt to impose global rule by so-called experts.

Some social media companies are censoring our efforts to promote a seminar we hosted on the Hillsdale campus that explored the topic of the Great Reset. Fortunately, we can communicate directly with you.

In this seminar, we...
* Consider the idea of the Great Reset and its implications for America.
* Explore how the ideology of "woke capitalism" is infiltrating large corporations.
* Delve into pro-freedom alternatives to this tyrannical plan.

**Sign up now using the secure link below, and we 'll send the Great Reset seminar lectures--absolutely free--directly to your inbox.**

[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/the-great-reset-cca/](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=0d74c95fbd4d4102a7d20a9b22700207&_e=3gCfrM7zOHSnRdr8Q4gIWJ-ae-p666yTUA0wM4yfHvGhlqt4R0Kf1V0GXQe2LUn88KZ03WGnsOThxJGVtW7txTwBk7NIPmblWgSVkToY3SfvRBgsBbWnrLEX-yREw6XTxdayNuqdulOYkeL4mxVBmUUZE8FleYTZeiLyOBPkWh-NHoFGy8Lj6F9ognCgmy_JafTYbwc3Kd0WIS4yUqF-fj9XsGMA-fZAQvnyMolhDuJGjo5qfErHoI4-fzKNHSzagek7cEIXWb32BtuWjjTAccvwaofcFqBrmLQy5AWGWOUwDz_Wx350J-XTo-nKhk0lzGf0A3fzvC036rmK66uaFXvnhFSQIjWt3f82WxOH2gaaN_boXnGJON9M7Pv1A5TdhPdTFBrVW7N0kQZlW56pDA%3D%3D)

Don't miss this opportunity to learn lessons that are essential to the defense of American liberty.

Use the link above to receive your Great Reset video series today.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

name: Jacky Rosen
subject: This is my story
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rosen for Nevada
body:
how I first got involved in politics
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85707562/475633705/1514755465?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk3838-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMzkyNmJjMS0zMDFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GVtsuLpYfgp0ppBtPM6v_BZR7h_L2Ea8nAkkSFCdzk8=)
Can I tell you the story of how I first got involved in politics, friend?
While serving as the president of Nevada's largest synagogue, I was recruited to run in a competitive House race to help flip a red seat blue. Looking back, it's incredibly fitting that _this_ was my entry point to public service – **because my Judaism helps guide much of my work in the Senate today.** **In fact, my decision to say "yes" and run for public office in the first place was guided by the Jewish principle of Tikkun Olam – which means to leave the world a better place than how we found it.**
Now, as the third Jewish woman and only former synagogue president in Senate history, I'm proud to serve Nevada and our country.** ** And as we face everything from growing antisemitism to attacks on reproductive freedom to threats to tear down our very democracy, I'm as committed as ever to keep fighting to better this world and make positive change happen. **But I need your help.**
**My ****far-right opponent**** set a new GOP fundraising record to defeat me, and recent polling shows me tied in the race that experts say will likely decide Senate control. So please, I'm humbly asking:**
[**If the principle of Tikkun Olam resonates with you ****– if you're with me in the fight to continue making progress for the people and communities that we love – ****will**** you chip in $5 or more today to help me defend my seat in Nevada and our Democratic Senate majority?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85707563/475633706/1514755465?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&refcode=jtk3838-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMzkyNmJjMS0zMDFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GVtsuLpYfgp0ppBtPM6v_BZR7h_L2Ea8nAkkSFCdzk8=)

clean_body: how I first got involved in politics | | | = Can I tell you the story of how I first got involved in politics, friend? While serving as the president of Nevada's largest synagogue, I was recruited to run in a competitive House race to help flip a red seat blue. Looking back, it's incredibly fitting that this was my entry point to public service – because my Judaism helps guide much of my work in the Senate today. In fact, my decision to say "yes" and run for public office in the first place was guided by the Jewish principle of Tikkun Olam – which means to leave the world a better place than how we found it. Now, as the third Jewish woman and only former synagogue president in Senate history, I'm proud to serve Nevada and our country. And as we face everything from growing antisemitism to attacks on reproductive freedom to threats to tear down our very democracy, I'm as committed as ever to keep fighting to better this world and make positive change happen. But I need your help. My far-right opponent set a new GOP fundraising record to defeat me, and recent polling shows me tied in the race that experts say will likely decide Senate control. So please, I'm humbly asking: = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = Thank you so much, Jacky | | = A note from Sen. Jacky Rosen:** Friend, I’m fighting tirelessly in the Senate to deliver progress for people across Nevada and our country. But experts say I’m one of the most vulnerable senators up for reelection, and the GOP is zeroing in on me as a top target for defeat. That’s why I’m counting on your critical grassroots support to fight back and win. Senate control will all come down to Nevada – and I need to know you’re with me. = You can also to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive updates from my campaign. name: The Office of Delegate Briana D. Sewell
subject: Del. Sewell's May 27th Newsletter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Briana Sewell
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/New%20Sewell%20Delegate%20Logo%20.png]
May 27th Newsletter
Dear neighbors,
On this Memorial Day, we express gratitude for those who lost their lives serving and defending our country, and say thank you for your service to those who served our country or are currently serving. Whatever your plans for this day, I hope you include time to honor the brave service men and women to whom Memorial Day is dedicated. On a lighter note, I hope you enjoy the unofficial beginning of summer with family and friends and you appreciate a few General Assembly updates before your log off and to enjoy the weather.
General Assembly Updates
Prince William County Virtual Town Hall | June 4 @7pm: Join me and members of the PWC General Assembly delegation for a moderated zoom town hall on June 4th! Please RSVP here [https://www.facebook.com/events/1514101985806845/] and submit your questions in advance here [https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScGkqA35Bw3uaXzuCctaP_bFexH3d2y3YiuBzX-g_QPph4nqQ/viewform] . [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-05-23%20at%201.39.13%20PM.png]
VA Housing Commission (VHC) Update [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/1691772445907.jpeg]
On Monday, May 20th, I participated in the Virginia Housing Commission (VHC) Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) Committee meeting where I serve as one of the member representatives. The purpose of the VHC is to “study and provide recommendations to ensure and foster the availability of safe, sound affordable housing for every Virginian." Specifically, the ADU work group is tasked with looking at effective legislative solutions to legalizing and regulating accessory dwelling units which are secondary dwelling units built on the property of a primary dwelling.
These ADUs can be more affordable, provide opportunity for older adults to live nearby their families, and allow for single family homeowners to make additional income. Monday’s meeting provided an overview of the literature on affordable dwelling units and offered a wide-ranging discussion about what localities are doing right now in relation to ADU’s and what possible legislation may do to change the legal landscape related to this type of housing. You can watch the full meeting here [https://vhc.virginia.gov/documents/2024/VAHousingMay2024.mp4] and learn more about the Virginia Housing Commission here [https://vhc.virginia.gov/meetings.asp] .
Issue of the Week - Department of Motor Vehicles Legislation
Now that the Commonwealth has a budget, we return to our explanation of these bills we passed in the most recent legislative session and discuss the ones that will go into effect on July 1st - only a few short weeks away. This week our focus is transportation. I will provide an overview of all bills that passed through the Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) subcommittee, which is one of the subcommittees I have the privilege of chairing in the House of Delegates.
Many of the bills in the DMV subcommittee come directly from folks who work at the DMV, and throughout their work, have identified challenges in process or lack of clarity in current law. These bills are affectionately known as “agency bills” and many of the bills I will describe below fall into this category. For example, two bills (HB 844 and HB 425) dealt with bringing Virginia law into compliance with the federal code and thereby maintaining our federal funding. Other bills focus on creating efficiencies for the work of the DMV. For example, HB845 makes it easier for the DMV to deal with abandoned vehicles by allowing them to use a national database to verify lien-holder and title information instead of going through individual localities. Similarly, HB1409 allows the release of title information for auto crashes to law enforcement as well as the DMV in case of an accident and HB1112 clarifies databases that may be accessed by the DMV for certain information.
Many of the DMV bills focus on making life easier for residents of Virginia. HB1538 allows a manufactured home owner to de-title their manufactured home to convert the home to real property, while HB1419 allows businesses like Carvana to sell cars fully virtually by allowing a digital titling process. HB1084 allows residents with temporary disabilities to get a disability placard for up to a year if their medical provider thinks it will be necessary, saving money and additional trips to the DMV. HB 1163 clarifies that the basis for DMV medical review should be related to having a disability related to driving a vehicle, not just stereotypes related to disability to prevent discrimination against disabled drivers. HB1224 allows members of faith communities who by their religious beliefs do not take photos the ability to access a non-photo ID at a lower cost. I was especially proud that my bill, HB1526, allows electric utilities to restore power more quickly in case of a storm by raising the weight limit on certain vehicles and enabling single crews to load a full set of poles and materials on a single vehicle.
Finally, several bills dealt with the negotiations between auto dealers and manufacturers. HB191 attempted to increase the predictability and objectivity of negotiations between parties, while HB1106 allows auto dealers who are becoming recertified to skip an in-person class and go straight to an exam if they feel prepared. HB1107 allows the DMV to establish the cost of a field visit for a required examination of an independent auto dealer when examiners are sent to the site and the dealer is repeatedly not available for the assessment.
News You Can Use
Governor Youngkin takes action on final bills from 2024 General Assembly session [https://www.wavy.com/news/governor-youngkin-takes-action-on-final-bills-from-2024-general-assembly-session/] | WAVY.com
Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin has taken action on the final batch of bills from the 2024 General Assembly Session.
U.S. Supreme Court won’t hear Mountain Valley Pipeline eminent domain case [https://cardinalnews.org/2024/05/20/u-s-supreme-court-wont-hear-mountain-valley-pipeline-eminent-domain-case/] | Cardinal News
Six Southwest Virginia landowners have argued for years that Congress unconstitutionally gave a federal agency the power to allow pipeline developers to seize their land. The case already came before the Supreme Court once.
Rural Virginia sees same population growth rate as Nashville. That growth just isn’t evenly distributed. [https://cardinalnews.org/2024/05/20/rural-virginia-sees-same-population-growth-rate-as-nashville-that-growth-just-isnt-evenly-distributed/] | Cardinal News
New census figures show rural Virginia is now gaining population faster than rural North Carolina.
Youngkin vetoes bills on contraception access, skill games, Confederate heritage rollbacks [https://www.insidenova.com/headlines/youngkin-vetoes-bills-on-contraception-access-skill-games-confederate-heritage-rollbacks/article_8ccba882-cea7-5673-9802-3f8aff6e42dd.html] | InsideNOVA
Last week, Gov. Glenn Youngkin vetoed 48 more bills passed by the Democratic-led General Assembly, blocking legislation aimed at prerving contraception access, ending state perks for Confederate heritage groups and legalizing slot machine lookalikes known as skill games.
Bill to teach high school students how to reverse opioid overdose advances [https://www.pilotonline.com/2024/02/08/bill-to-teach-high-school-students-how-to-reverse-opioid-overdose-advances/] | Virginian-Pilot
Del. Briana Sewell, D-Woodbridge, introduced House Bill 732 that requires all public schools to have a two-dose supply of naloxone by the 2026-2027 school year. At least one school nurse or other employee must be trained in its administration.
In the Community
Prince William County Memorial Day Commemoration: Every year I attend the Prince William County memorial day ceremony to commemorate the brave men and women of the armed forces who have lost their lives. We remember their sacrifice and honor their memory through our acts. To those in our Prince William veteran community, we thank you for your service. [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/MemorialDay.jpeg]
Prince William County Public Safety Valor Awards: Team Sewell was proud to attend the 2024 Valor Awards presented by the Prince William County Chamber of Commerce. It was exceptionally moving to learn the many stories of bravery and selflessness of our public safety community here in Prince William County. While private citizens like many of us run from danger, these exceptional individuals run towards it and many of them risk their lives and limb on a daily basis to protect and serve community members. Thank you to all in our public safety community for your service. [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/IMG_1343.jpg]
Prince William Public Schools Retirement Ceremony: Last week, an exceptional group of educators in Prince William County celebrated their retirement from our public schools. These teachers, administrative personnel, and others have dedicated decades to the raising of our youth in partnership with our families, many impacting several generations of residents. I am so grateful to them and wish them a restful and rewarding retirement. You can watch the full retirement ceremony here [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ui9QcnxrY1E] . [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-05-23%20at%203.05.37%20PM.png]
Community Events
May 25 | Community Pools & Waterparks Open For Summer 2024 [https://www.pwcva.gov/events/community-pools-waterparks-open-today]
Prince William County has three outdoor community pools, plus Veterans Park Pool which is an outdoor community pool with a twisting waterslide. Visit https://www.pwcva.gov/pools [https://www.pwcva.gov/pools] for more information.
May 28 | #PWCPD EVENT: Community Watch Presentation [https://www.pwcva.gov/events/pwcpd-event-community-watch-presentation]
Learn how you can work with the Police Department to make your neighborhood safer. Hosted at the Potomac Community Library, 2201 Opitz Blvd., WOODBRIDGE - VA.
May 29 | Human Trafficking: Know Your Rights [https://www.pwcva.gov/events/human-trafficking-know-your-rights]
Join this free, virtual webinar on Human Trafficking hosted by the Prince William County Human Rights Office. You will learn about: EEOC, human trafficking and legal enforcement, youth human trafficking prevention, intervention and resources, and underlying causes of human trafficking from patterns of discrimination.
June 1 | Zumba Pride at Chinn Aquatics & Fitness Center [https://www.pwcva.gov/events/zumba-pride-chinn-aquatics-fitness-center]
Dance at Chinn Aquatics and Fitness Center in this celebration of pride and diversity. This event is FREE to passholders and a $12 group exercise drop-in rate applies for non-passholders.
June 5 | Addressing Food Insecurity Through Financial Empowerment [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/addressing-food-insecurity-through-financial-empowerment-tickets-904832086127?aff=ebdssbdestsearch]
Jamaur Law Foundation Inc and The Prince William County Community Foundation, Inc.’s “SMART MONEY" financial literacy program is designed to equip you with the tools and knowledge you need to take control of your finances and ensure your family has access to nutritious food.
June 8 | Fire & Rescue Life Safety Information & Education Pop Up [https://www.pwcva.gov/events/fire-rescue-life-safety-information-education-pop-1]
The Prince William County Fire & Rescue System is hosting Fire and Life Safety information and education pop up at West Gate Elementary School and will cover their Smoke Alarm Program, File of Life Program, when to call 911, and emergency preparedness.
June 19 | 4th Annual Greater PW Juneteenth Parade! [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/4th-annual-greater-pw-juneteenth-parade-tickets-888467087977?aff=ebdssbdestsearch]
Celebrate freedom and unity at this year's "4th Annual Greater PW Juneteenth Parade" held at the Prince William Forest Park!
State and Community Info & Resources
Virginia Cooperative Extension | This arm of our county government helps people put scientific knowledge to work through learning experiences that improve economic, environmental, and social well-being. Educational programs are designed to enable families to better manage resources, help youth become productive members of society, and improve the impact of urban horticulture on the environment. Learn more about the Prince William VCE at https://www.pwcva.gov/department/virginia-cooperative-extension [https://www.pwcva.gov/department/virginia-cooperative-extension] .
How to Get a Library Card | For those Prince William County and City of Manassas residents who do not yet have a library card, you can sign up for a digital library card or physical card. The digital library card allows access to the digital library without visiting a library branch. For a physical card, go to your nearest library branch and fill out a library card application. You’ll need proof of your current address which could be a lease with your name and street address or a driver’s license. Learn more here: https://www.pwcva.gov/department/library/get-library-card [https://www.pwcva.gov/department/library/get-library-card]
Veterans Benefits Support | The Virginia Department of Veterans Services advocates for Virginia veterans and connects them to benefits and services they have earned. Information on current federal, state and local veterans’ programs, entitlements and referral services is available in Virginia through a network of 35 benefit service offices. All services are provided free of charge. To view the DVS benefits brochure click here [https://www.dvs.virginia.gov/benefits#:~:text=benefits%20brochure%20click-,here,-.%20To%20View] . To view the DVS benefits video click here [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nWOkXyPzG5U&t=63s] .
Stay In Touch
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-05-23%20at%203.54.45%20PM.png]
Thanks again for reading our weekly update and staying in touch with our office. If there is anything we can help you with, don’t hesitate to call our office at 703-261-4787 or send us an email at DelBSewell@house.virginia.gov [DelBSewell@house.virginia.gov] .
In Service,
Briana Sewell
Paid for by Friends of Briana Sewell.
Friends of Briana Sewell
PO Box 1849
Woodbridge, VA 22195
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/BDPKPY_4S0izjMklQV7G1w2?unsubscribedata=AaOZxTZSYdjP4vm9fTOW5T%2FC7W5bshxzD%2FhRZQtKutulORPo0cc50ZWtZiWseP0pnp4lWQCmvu8F%2FQB5zw3YNPqNaNQlU4ovOrdcGcu2IKJnKezOewEJ60WruTFjJDdBX6pxbz1POU31n9AKheTLZ%2BdXSMdyU0UKgVrik%2Bx6CKMtAj5FtSePydYSVPH5XJjzcOqn3SJv7GyLj9GrNfnILhYAhvDUJqxJobb1J1oS9Kt%2FQlbeDNCFS3eTG7BI4yEep9LL4UChRpPa5sJ%2BLffbIjKYB2aHBFmeKlkMtKA%2FLguptvSeCtf1xwJER7r3H26NILB8QYthh75ukMlpSTUM2ugSecxqBw%2B35LoNuelXseK4hTOpOiYey9pvwUNe7AhWTNw72qug26bpJUQmkutNV5Yb%2BlVeegOMo332tEArN2LwlB%2FflXAzA%2B7M4ZQ5GYvs .

I was especially proud that my bill, HB1526, allows electric utilities to restore power more quickly in case of a storm by raising the weight limit on certain vehicles and enabling single crews to load a full set of poles and materials on a single vehicle. Finally, several bills dealt with the negotiations between auto dealers and manufacturers. HB191 attempted to increase the predictability and objectivity of negotiations between parties, while HB1106 allows auto dealers who are becoming recertified to skip an in-person class and go straight to an exam if they feel prepared. HB1107 allows the DMV to establish the cost of a field visit for a required examination of an independent auto dealer when examiners are sent to the site and the dealer is repeatedly not available for the assessment. News You Can Use Governor Youngkin takes action on final bills from 2024 General Assembly session [] | WAVY.com Virginia Governor Glenn Youngkin has taken action on the final batch of bills from the 2024 General Assembly Session. U.S. Supreme Court won’t hear Mountain Valley Pipeline eminent domain case [] | Cardinal News Six Southwest Virginia landowners have argued for years that Congress unconstitutionally gave a federal agency the power to allow pipeline developers to seize their land. The case already came before the Supreme Court once. Rural Virginia sees same population growth rate as Nashville. That growth just isn’t evenly distributed. [] | Cardinal News New census figures show rural Virginia is now gaining population faster than rural North Carolina. Youngkin vetoes bills on contraception access, skill games, Confederate heritage rollbacks [] | InsideNOVA Last week, Gov. Glenn Youngkin vetoed 48 more bills passed by the Democratic-led General Assembly, blocking legislation aimed at prerving contraception access, ending state perks for Confederate heritage groups and legalizing slot machine lookalikes known as skill games. Bill to teach high school students how to reverse opioid overdose advances [] | Virginian-Pilot Del. Briana Sewell, D-Woodbridge, introduced House Bill 732 that requires all public schools to have a two-dose supply of naloxone by the 2026-2027 school year. At least one school nurse or other employee must be trained in its administration. In the Community Prince William County Memorial Day Commemoration: Every year I attend the Prince William County memorial day ceremony to commemorate the brave men and women of the armed forces who have lost their lives. We remember their sacrifice and honor their memory through our acts. To those in our Prince William veteran community, we thank you for your service. [] Prince William County Public Safety Valor Awards: Team Sewell was proud to attend the 2024 Valor Awards presented by the Prince William County Chamber of Commerce. It was exceptionally moving to learn the many stories of bravery and selflessness of our public safety community here in Prince William County. While private citizens like many of us run from danger, these exceptional individuals run towards it and many of them risk their lives and limb on a daily basis to protect and serve community members. Thank you to all in our public safety community for your service. [] Prince William Public Schools Retirement Ceremony: Last week, an exceptional group of educators in Prince William County celebrated their retirement from our public schools. These teachers, administrative personnel, and others have dedicated decades to the raising of our youth in partnership with our families, many impacting several generations of residents. I am so grateful to them and wish them a restful and rewarding retirement. You can watch the full retirement ceremony here [] . [] Community Events May 25 | Community Pools & Waterparks Open For Summer 2024 [] Prince William County has three outdoor community pools, plus Veterans Park Pool which is an outdoor community pool with a twisting waterslide. Visit [] for more information. May 28 | #PWCPD EVENT: Community Watch Presentation [] Learn how you can work with the Police Department to make your neighborhood safer. Hosted at the Potomac Community Library, 2201 Opitz Blvd., WOODBRIDGE - VA. May 29 | Human Trafficking: Know Your Rights [] Join this free, virtual webinar on Human Trafficking hosted by the Prince William County Human Rights Office. You will learn about: EEOC, human trafficking and legal enforcement, youth human trafficking prevention, intervention and resources, and underlying causes of human trafficking from patterns of discrimination. June 1 | Zumba Pride at Chinn Aquatics & Fitness Center [] Dance at Chinn Aquatics and Fitness Center in this celebration of pride and diversity. This event is FREE to passholders and a $12 group exercise drop-in rate applies for non-passholders. June 5 | Addressing Food Insecurity Through Financial Empowerment [] Jamaur Law Foundation Inc and The Prince William County Community Foundation, Inc.’s “SMART MONEY" financial literacy program is designed to equip you with the tools and knowledge you need to take control of your finances and ensure your family has access to nutritious food. June 8 | Fire & Rescue Life Safety Information & Education Pop Up [] The Prince William County Fire & Rescue System is hosting Fire and Life Safety information and education pop up at West Gate Elementary School and will cover their Smoke Alarm Program, File of Life Program, when to call 911, and emergency preparedness. June 19 | 4th Annual Greater PW Juneteenth Parade! [] Celebrate freedom and unity at this year's "4th Annual Greater PW Juneteenth Parade" held at the Prince William Forest Park! State and Community Info & Resources Virginia Cooperative Extension | This arm of our county government helps people put scientific knowledge to work through learning experiences that improve economic, environmental, and social well-being. Educational programs are designed to enable families to better manage resources, help youth become productive members of society, and improve the impact of urban horticulture on the environment. Learn more about the Prince William VCE at [] . How to Get a Library Card | For those Prince William County and City of Manassas residents who do not yet have a library card, you can sign up for a digital library card or physical card. The digital library card allows access to the digital library without visiting a library branch. For a physical card, go to your nearest library branch and fill out a library card application. You’ll need proof of your current address which could be a lease with your name and street address or a driver’s license. Learn more here: [] Veterans Benefits Support | The Virginia Department of Veterans Services advocates for Virginia veterans and connects them to benefits and services they have earned. Information on current federal, state and local veterans’ programs, entitlements and referral services is available in Virginia through a network of 35 benefit service offices. All services are provided free of charge. To view the DVS benefits brochure click here [] . To view the DVS benefits video click here [] . Stay In Touch [] Thanks again for reading our weekly update and staying in touch with our office. If there is anything we can help you with, don’t hesitate to call our office at 703-261-4787 or send us an email at DelBSewell@house.virginia.gov [DelBSewell@house.virginia.gov] . In Service, Briana Sewell Paid for by Friends of Briana Sewell. Friends of Briana Sewell PO Box 1849 Woodbridge, VA 22195 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Derek Marshall
subject: Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Derek Marshall for Congress
body:
| | |
As we gather to commemorate Memorial Day, I am reminded of the profound sacrifices made by the brave men and women who have served our nation. Today, we honor those who have laid down their lives in defense of our freedoms, our democracy, and the values we hold dear. Their courage and dedication remind us of the true cost of liberty.
Memorial Day is more than a day of remembrance; it is a call to action. We must ensure that the families of our fallen heroes receive the support and care they deserve. This includes providing comprehensive benefits, quality healthcare, and educational opportunities for their loved ones. As a community, we owe it to them to make sure they are never forgotten and always supported.
On this solemn day, let us also reflect on the principles of justice, equality, and democracy for which so many have given their lives. As a congressional nominee, I pledge to uphold these values and work tirelessly to create a nation that is worthy of their sacrifice.
Let us come together, not just in memory, but in action. Let us strive to build a future that honors their legacy through our commitment to peace, justice, and opportunity for all.
Thank you, and may we never forget the brave souls who have given everything for our country.
-Derek [Donate Now](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvkP_qkVIdE665h6yyoYaT94bUOOIQgnkwo900aHwFGEVTZ2eUuJrYuV4lh8mX0PCcc-Pmzq9vhhQdzZuGP1b9o6hoUkQxOqOuQLiAwy1SAboLLkergIEGcAGlVfT75l69BG5dcAdkQ2l2c3rmWYnNT0_wieGznrkkdbw4VtjLDYEyvnRdMxCXIB557iTjwWPUdag0QCB0OvP7uHZtRK51cqymoj7kyeZS1FgkujY19jilMTOLvRsQDqzfTGW8eB4MT7PpukqGHbW-WsEWBJ94aZjcfyFuGTLLs0k-hDa81juwenMKzK9FryRi_H2nBkvU/46p/Rg4suKHuRjyuO0KBQaaDag/h0/h001.lzKXcI2jg_tOeat9Dzqb-yys3148QK0k0fYQUEwqaUc)

Happy Memorial Day, folks. Today, I'm thinking of the countless Americans – especially those from Florida's 23rd – who served in the Armed Forces and paid the ultimate sacrifice. They put their lives on the line to protect our hard-fought freedoms and rights – words could never adequately express our gratitude. And to every Gold Star family: our appreciation and honor runs deep for the sacrifices you've made, too. The loss of a loved one is never easy, but we ought to be comforted by the selflessness and bravery of your loved ones. With gratitude, \- Jared Moskowitz

name: Becca Balint
subject: Commemorating Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont
body:
Today, we honor and remember the brave Americans who have laid down their lives to defend our country’s most sacred principles.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85708636/475637631/966991095?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYmUxMDUyNy0zMjFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Cd16Ox_crNcf8GvDFp6vL3xUNofTccuEWZEg4v-TzEs=&emci=b2b6885b-7118-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=dbe10527-321c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=43402272) | Hi Peter, Today, we honor and remember the brave Americans who have laid down their lives to defend our country’s most sacred principles: Freedom, unity, democracy, and liberty and justice for all. _But here’s the truth:_ Remembering those who’ve served and sacrificed for our country isn’t enough. Many veterans return home from service carrying scars – both visible and invisible. The difficulty of transitioning back into civilian life means that a high number of our service members grapple with mental health issues, substance use disorders, homelessness, and high rates of suicide – often without the support they need to navigate these challenges. As Memorial Day coincides with the end of Mental Health Awareness Month, I want you to know that I’m not just committed to remembering those who’ve served:**I’m committed to investing more resources into mental health care, helping veterans re-enter the workforce, and fighting to secure the funding that the VA needs. ** Every day, but especially today, we must thank all military members and their families for their service and sacrifice. And if you or someone you know is struggling or could use support, I urge you to call the Veterans Crisis Line by dialing 988 and pressing 1. Thank you, Becca Balint
---

* * *

Please, we need 10,000 signatures by midnight to demonstrate massive support! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/69215?t=7&akid=14824%2E878179%2EPf0uGw) | [Donate $3 to sign the letter now »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/69215?t=8&akid=14824%2E878179%2EPf0uGw)

TO: President Biden
FROM: A grateful Democrat
Subject: I’m so grateful.

**Dear President Biden,

I’m so thankful for everything you’ve done as President!

**Despite MAGA turmoil, over the last three years, you’ve fought TIRELESSLY for the American people — defending abortion rights, confirming an incredible Supreme Court Justice, saving Social Security from GOP cuts, and creating millions of jobs for the people.**

I know standing up to MAGA Republican attacks isn’t easy, but it is always the right thing to do.

So as the 2024 race kicks into high gear, I wanted to show my support and thank you for being such an amazing leader for our country!

[**I hope you know that there are MILLIONS of us across the nation who stand with you. That’s why I’m signing this letter and chipping in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to thank you and to keep electing incredible Democrats up and down the ballot »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/69215?t=9&akid=14824%2E878179%2EPf0uGw)** | [SPACE RUNNING OUT: Donate $3 to sign now »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/69215?t=10&akid=14824%2E878179%2EPf0uGw)

name: Scott Fitzgerald
subject: Happy Memorial Day
body:
| | |
---
Happy Memorial Day, friend! As we pause to reflect on this solemn day, I find myself deeply moved by the tremendous sacrifice of our fallen soldiers. Their sacrifices are the foundation of our freedom. May we never forget the sacrifices made for us and always strive to honor those who have given so much. Thank you, and God Bless America. **Scott Fitzgerald**

Scott Fitzgerald | | | | | | | ---|--- | | Use of military images or rank does not imply endorsement by the U.S. Department of Defense or the U.S. Army

name: Wes Moore
subject: Today, let’s honor the sacrifice of those who gave their lives to serve this country
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85709662/475640562/-926250309?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1ZTcxNWQ4Yy0zMzFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tlYRvvkX168jgCYyxsrLE8WSOHcYkTy1JHwfoxmxbsM=&emci=a79c8b45-2e1c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=5e715d8c-331c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=24635485)
Friend, this day took on a very different meaning for me after my deployment in 2005. Today, and every Memorial Day, we must remember and honor the men and women who gave their lives in service to this country. When I was deployed to Afghanistan as a member of the 82nd Airborne Division of the U.S. Army, I was honored to stand shoulder-to-shoulder with some of the best soldiers in the world. It forged the strongest friendships I have ever experienced. But unfortunately, not all of my brothers and sisters made it home. There were some who struggled with mental health and lost their lives to the invisible wounds of war. We owe an unpayable debt to their families. **I will never forget their sacrifice.** Today, make “Thank you for your service,” a meaningful act, the beginning of a conversation and connection. Not just a perfunctory gesture. My heart goes out to the families of those who gave the ultimate sacrifice and gave their lives to serve this country. Elevate, Wes
---

This is a HUGE bombshell, Peter.

Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito has been _**EXPOSED**_ for showing his allegiance to the far-right insurrectionist movement at his own home by hoisting up an upside down flag – the symbol of the “Stop the Steal” movement!

---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/what-will-it-take-for-the-usa-to?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store)
---
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..j06e1ceS5O71F_-6gx-vPGdgCwj8Cg8KdlOBfbqSOdE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=145024643)
Do enough people yet notice how the pundits outraged over the fabricated story of 40 beheaded babies last October seem to have nothing to say about the horrifying reality of beheaded Palestinian babies in Rafah happening right now?
Three days ago the International Court of Justice ordered Israel to stop its invasion and bombardment of Rafah, [ruling](https://substack.com/redirect/62142f69-0579-4428-a06d-2b4236d6f5f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> "Israel must immediately halt its military offensive, and any other action in the Rafah Governorate" which could bring about "the physical destruction" of the Palestinians - alluding to what constitutes genocide under international law.
Minutes after the ruling was issued, the Israeli military defied the ICJ and launched another round of deadly bombings on Rafah. Yesterday, that devastation reached new levels as Israeli drone strikes on already displaced persons camp in Rafah killed at least 44 innocent people, mostly women and children. As Amnesty International [reports](https://substack.com/redirect/7c6d686d-8c7a-43f5-9214-6b925b30d994?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> The International Criminal Court should investigate as war crimes three Israeli air strikes that killed 44 Palestinian civilians, including 32 children. The strikes are further evidence of a broader pattern of war crimes committed by the Israeli military in the occupied Gaza Strip in the last seven months.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..j06e1ceS5O71F_-6gx-vPGdgCwj8Cg8KdlOBfbqSOdE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=145024643)
Meanwhile, Palestinians continue to suffer famine. The UN has stopped sending aid due to incessant Israeli bombing, and the Department of Defense reports that zero of 569 metric tons of aid delivered to Gaza has reached starving Palestinians. Contrary to claims that Hamas is blocking the aid, U.S. officials confirm it is not being blocked by Hamas. Instead, [multiple sources report that extremist Israeli settlers are blocking the aid](https://substack.com/redirect/7e2ea43c-9a5d-4cb3-96c7-28d9f1735e1e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), after being tipped off by the Israeli military itself.
> Those blocking the vehicles say the aid they carry is being diverted by Hamas instead of being delivered to civilians in need, a claim relief agencies reject. US officials have also said that Israel has offered no evidence to support allegations that Hamas is diverting aid.
Recall that when Yemenis blocked ships to prevent genocide they were bombed. Now, as Israelis block aid to enable famine, they're ignored. The hypocrisy and white supremacy in our foreign policy is deadly.
And this is part of a deadly pattern that has spanned the entirety of Israel's bombing since October 7, at least. Five months ago in December, 2023, the New York Times [reported](https://substack.com/redirect/187d24c7-fb2c-47d8-83c3-c76166b242a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> Israel claims it protects civilians, but a New York Times investigation found that at least 200 times Israel dropped its most destructive bombs in areas it designated as safe for civilians in Gaza.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..j06e1ceS5O71F_-6gx-vPGdgCwj8Cg8KdlOBfbqSOdE?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=145024643)
Another investigation by NBC [reached the same conclusion](https://substack.com/redirect/a0e307c7-537b-476f-87d7-33cb2edd112a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Let's be clear, hundreds of bombings on those locations you designated as safe zones, and forced people to move to for their safety before bombing them is not an accident--it's intentional policy. This is one reason why ICC prosecutor Karim Khan has charged Israeli leadership with war crimes.
Indeed, at this point the question is not whether Israel is committing war crimes--that's already been well established by numerous independent organizations. The question is, how much longer will the United States allow itself to become isolated on the global stage to protect Israel from legal accountability for its ongoing war crimes?
Germany this week, who is one of America's closest allies and staunchest defenders of Israel, finally capitulated and admitted Israel is committing indefensible war crimes. German Vice Chancellor Habeck condemns [declared](https://substack.com/redirect/bb546c3f-cc78-4161-aa17-2d4096d2b9db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> The famine, the suffering of the Palestinians, the attacks in the Gaza Strip are incompatible with international law. It is indeed the case that Israel has crossed a line there, and it must not do so.
Ireland, Norway, and Spain -- three more close allies of the United States -- have declared their recognition of Palestine as a sovereign, as has the majority of the UN member nations. Meanwhile, in bombing the displaced person's refugee camp in Rafah, Israel bombed yet another location they'd previously declared a safe zone.
The United States can preach as much as they want about the need for international rules based order, decency, and humanity, but actions speak louder than words. The United States hasn't merely stood by, they've actively funded Israeli bombing to the tune of tens of billions of dollars. Until the United States is willing to uphold justice and international law, willing to ensure Israel faces accountability for its war crimes, and stop funding Israeli bombing, its empty words ring hollow and represent glaring hypocrisy. As Americans, we must demand better of our elected officials because this status quo is intolerable.
Indeed, to drive the point home even more, how American politicians are treating Palestinian civilians, is how they may well treat us should they be given the opportunity
At least 32 innocent Palestinian children are dead due to Israeli bombs funded by US tax dollars. Today, nearly a million displaced Palestinians remain in Rafah, with nowhere else to go, knowing any given second could be their last. What will it take for Americans to stand up to our elected officials en masse and demand, Never Again?
**Note: I encourage readers to join me in[contributing to Humanity First UK](https://substack.com/redirect/1326dfb4-5186-4a0e-b76e-431248fc348b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), who has worked in Gaza for decades and currently has physicians and healthcare workers on the ground in and around Rafah.**

name: Matt Diemer
subject: Remembering the fallen on Memorial Day
body:
| |
---
Peter, On this Memorial Day, I want to take a moment to express my thanks to the brave men and women who have served and sacrificed for our great nation. Their dedication and courage have shaped the very fabric of our freedom. As we pay tribute to those who have fallen, let us also renew our commitment to ensuring they receive the respect, support, and care they deserve. Their sacrifices should never be forgotten. May we honor our heroes not just in words but in deeds, by working toward a future where their service is met with the unwavering support they've earned.
---
Sincerely, Matt Diemer Democratic Candidate for Congress OH-7
---
|
---
Diemer for Congress P.O. Box 40042

# FROM THE DESK OF LARRY KUDLOW
---
| | Peter,**I am pleased to share with you that The New York Sun, one of my daily must-read papers, is now the exclusive publisher of my regular columns.

I invite you to consider joining the Sun to receive unlimited access to my latest commentary, alongside some of the finest American journalism.

**But there's also far more to what the Sun is building.

As you know, journalism isn't what it used to be.

I've been in the business for decades, and never before have I seen this once noble profession so abused. The major papers are no longer on the side of the American people, and we are right to withhold our trust.

The impact on our democracy is considerable. Without real accountability, the worst actors in government and at corporate institutions have free reign, and patriotic, hard-working Americans are being sidelined and ignored.

The antidote is to join the movement to build a better alternative. That's the American way.

**The charge to restore American journalism is being led by the Sun.** A proudly all-American, principled publication that has plenty of room for conservative thought and conservative writers, the Sun has upheld the highest journalistic standards since 1833. That's 18 years before the New York Times published its first number.**My decision for the New York Sun to become the exclusive publisher of my regular columns was not taken lightly.**

I value their work and have been impressed by their team, but I know they have a hell of a battle ahead. They are the David facing many Goliaths.

They will only succeed if we join with them in this grassroots national effort. Joining the Sun costs as little as a penny a day. I encourage you to sign up today. Every single person counts.
---

name: With Honor Action
subject: Face the Nation For Country Caucus Memorial Day Interview
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/bd70fb3f7bd3/invitation-two-parties-two-veterans-one-conversation-rep-lalota-rep-ryan-special-session-with-president-carter-alumni-13540213?e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | |
---
| | | | Dear Friends and Colleagues,

We shared with you on Friday, [this link ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=e881097b0e&e=33f53a5b1a)to a post with a short video we produced from the annual bipartisan war memorial cleaning that With Honor Action and the For Country Caucus organize. The video includes footage from cleaning the Vietnam Wall, a visit to the future GWOT Memorial site adjacent to the Wall, and powerful reflections from two of the surviving three bipartisan Vietnam veterans remaining in the U.S. House.

Yesterday, Margaret Brennan aired [an interview ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=e1bd889448&e=33f53a5b1a)on Face the Nation from our Vietnam Wall cleaning with two of the Members of the For Country Caucus. The segment is linked below and includes a reflection by both Members on deceased veterans they think about on Memorial Day. It also includes a focus a discussion of some of the top priorities With Honor Action and the For Country Caucus are working to complete this year, such as eliminating the unconscionable proliferation of military food insecurity in our junior ranks, in addition to longer-term priorities such as the expansion of voluntary national service.

Thank you, and thank you to those who made the ultimate sacrifice and their families.
---
| [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=f1598cf2e6&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | |
---
| Please visit With Honor's new website at [www.withhonor.org](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=45ffa34e03&e=33f53a5b1a) and consider forwarding this to a friend who may be interested in learning -- and doing -- more for our country.
---
| | | | | | [](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=57598910f7&e=33f53a5b1a)

| _With Honor Action fights polarization in Congress by supporting principled veterans across party lines who pledge to service with integrity, civility, and courage. With Honor Action works alongside the bipartisan For Country Caucus in Congress to pass critical legislation for our nation. clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | | | | Dear Friends and Colleagues, We shared with you on Friday, a post with a short video we produced from the annual bipartisan war memorial cleaning that With Honor Action and the For Country Caucus organize. The video includes footage from cleaning the Vietnam Wall, a visit to the future GWOT Memorial site adjacent to the Wall, and powerful reflections from two of the surviving three bipartisan Vietnam veterans remaining in the U.S. House. Yesterday, Margaret Brennan aired Face the Nation from our Vietnam Wall cleaning with two of the Members of the For Country Caucus. The segment is linked below and includes a reflection by both Members on deceased veterans they think about on Memorial Day. It also includes a focus a discussion of some of the top priorities With Honor Action and the For Country Caucus are working to complete this year, such as eliminating the unconscionable proliferation of military food insecurity in our junior ranks, in addition to longer-term priorities such as the expansion of voluntary national service. Thank you, and thank you to those who made the ultimate sacrifice and their families. | | | | | | Please visit With Honor's new website at and consider forwarding this to a friend who may be interested in learning -- and doing -- more for our country. | | | | | | | | | | | | PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC. | | With Honor Action fights polarization in Congress by supporting principled veterans across party lines who pledge to service with integrity, civility, and courage. With Honor Action works alongside the bipartisan For Country Caucus in Congress to pass critical legislation for our nation. Learn more about our work at WithHonor.org With Honor Action is a 501(c)(4), which serves as the organization’s policy and social-welfare arm. With Honor has an affiliated federally registered “super PAC” called With Honor Fund II. | © 2023 With Honor. All rights reserved. Our mailing address is: Suite 380 700 Pennsylvania Ave SE Washington, DC 20003 Want to change how you receive these emails? You can or .

name: Bob Casey
subject: “The last full measure of devotion”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BOB CASEY FOR SENATE INC
body:
We owe our fallen military, our veterans and their families a huge debt of gratitude.
# [](https://go.bobcasey.com/go/181?t=1&akid=3087%2E552475%2ENoNYKY)
Peter,
160 years ago today, the first Memorial Day was observed in Boalsburg, Pennsylvania to honor Union soldiers who gave their lives during the Civil War.
On this Memorial Day, I hope you’ll join me in remembering the brave souls who gave their lives in service to our Nation in what President Abraham Lincoln described as "the last full measure of devotion."
Today and every day, we owe our fallen military, our veterans, and their families a huge debt of gratitude, and I will always fight to ensure we fulfill our commitment to them.
With gratitude,
Bob Casey
**[Donate ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/casey-em-20240527-1?refcode=em20240527-1&refcode2=3087_552475_NoNYKY&akid=3087%2E552475%2ENoNYKY)**

* * *

name: Governor Scott Walker
subject: Jewish student denied entry
body:
Our hearts are breaking at the sight of the increasing unrest. | | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_s=454eea4b3b234ece8e2c663679dc6e0f&_t=xSTaSeTKi7DmiBhOX306TCoE2RHu3EQFjgi-xL4NBK8U332oZuKk28Xz3Jzl5hqSgb5yXl7sQDXY6_9fTXoQkDSM4CMrGCiIB78JRDvwNWM%3D)_
---
| | | [](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=454eea4b3b234ece8e2c663679dc6e0f&_e=NJucTGHFkPx2ly0nhGIPsZJrnJWOI2TPKoSxeGntF59qoO5Ct0RazboCGAAY7-dis8gO0OWNrK5crNdkNZx6Pm29q7VUE4iy_Amg1b8ZI_ZKKSjdMFBOPYPBb7V1-j5YIc4eooJQuyB9o9LbbWe9qtS2GmyocJME-2r_Q4CtGudKjrFxT0fZh-Y7NyPpYjU8NewiBS4kyM8hMYM5KkZefSH1Bez3H1SwxY-bMld4MvRakWjuOtERPWvBP6YEHFTmbbBfsXQYbxJhDLIrOJyu-qVro1xgK159MFJT3ngmUWa2b5sLrPFIVnyAIJqNNh73w__t4UMFs6oer8cFtmP8Eb_BvKmKPQmTfBXSBrhLCC1bJLnCGkf5UW44SARDl9j0YjucPWADEo3iXSvXRurJcg%3D%3D)
Fellow Patriot,

I'm sure by now you've seen the news about pro-Hamas agitators taking over university campuses. Our hearts are breaking at the sight of the increasing unrest.

But what the liberal media is not telling you are the ways that woke university administrators are siding with the radical Hamas sympathizers!

Just this week, a Jewish student at UCLA _was stopped from entering a college building because he is Jewish. _

Antisemitism like this has no place in America.

**[That's why Young America's Foundation is fighting back](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=454eea4b3b234ece8e2c663679dc6e0f&_e=NJucTGHFkPx2ly0nhGIPsZJrnJWOI2TPKoSxeGntF59qoO5Ct0RazboCGAAY7-dis8gO0OWNrK5crNdkNZx6Pm29q7VUE4iy_Amg1b8ZI_ZKKSjdMFBOPYPBb7V1-j5YIc4eooJQuyB9o9LbbWe9qtS2GmyocJME-2r_Q4CtGudKjrFxT0fZh-Y7NyPpYjU8NewiBS4kyM8hMYM5KkZefSH1Bez3H1SwxY-bMld4MvRUitGHgjKyDBvdqSu3PjQkMEpvM50RCWYdFTljHE_eja51qPTLRzn7IVXfMQpKE5EJcnXQGID98LVcSxD-z6QueGxCB1NIi3WWocmcrbiWWrPWNfB2wcML0oaxSZXSEWmhseJaoEffMeCF21qVjtz9CD7nfx5CyZSUNJsCNbhY0A%3D%3D)****and dispatching one of the world 's leading experts on Jihad, Robert Spencer, to UCLA to expose the truth about Hamas and its reign of terror.**

However, UCLA officials are stonewalling and trying to prevent the event from happening.

**Matthew Weinberg** , the chairman of the UCLA YAF chapter hosting Spencer, said, "The people in the encampments are frankly violating campus policy, and nothing is being done about it. People are being physically assaulted; we just want a free speech event, and we're being told no."

Imagine being a Jewish student and being told you can't hear from a preeminent Jewish foreign policy expert, but that pro-Hamas agitators can continue to threaten your safety.

Make no mistake, the current encampments of radical pro-Hamas agitators on university campuses are the direct result of _decades_ of woke, Leftist indoctrination by college administrators and professors. They have demonized conservative and Jewish voices, and now their indoctrination is bearing the bitter fruit of campus violence.

We need to raise the alarm _now_ before this unrest spreads.

**Specifically, we are looking for 100 patriots to step forward before midnight with a gift of $50 to Young America 's Foundation, so we can expose these UCLA administrators and every school administration that is cozying up to radical Jihad and Hamas. **

Your gift will help send leading conservative speakers like **Ben Shapiro** and **Robert Spencer** to campuses like UCLA so they can warn students about the lies and danger of Hamas. It will also support conservative and Jewish students on campuses by equipping them with conservative toolkits, pro-Israel resources, and legal protection from the universities that seek to suppress their First Amendment right to free speech.

**Can you make a gift today to support these students?** Here's a secure link where you can quickly make your gift and ensure that students can hear the truth from conservative experts before they fall victim to pro-Hamas lies: **[https://secure.yaf.org/expose-pro-hamas-college-administrators](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=454eea4b3b234ece8e2c663679dc6e0f&_e=NJucTGHFkPx2ly0nhGIPsZJrnJWOI2TPKoSxeGntF59qoO5Ct0RazboCGAAY7-dis8gO0OWNrK5crNdkNZx6Pm29q7VUE4iy_Amg1b8ZI_ZKKSjdMFBOPYPBb7V1-j5YIc4eooJQuyB9o9LbbWe9qtS2GmyocJME-2r_Q4CtGudKjrFxT0fZh-Y7NyPpYjU8NewiBS4kyM8hMYM5KkZefSH1Bez3H1SwxY-bMld4MvRa_Y0f1SK7MhFPqGQTTjXsf8vw_ESUZPAY56iKpfskkZxGAyc5SblPfZZW2nFagaSIEipwCGRn3iCUgENsJUhx0dwOhsg5cBijgzNbsIANgvxy441HxT4LuU6HrWPIIi7ireLU-9mn9tWEuwhfIOYSLl8YBSYGKEyw3d4y-lCdng%3D%3D)**

Thank you for standing with Jewish students like Matthew, who are fighting the growing madness on their campuses.

Keep Fighting for Freedom,

Governor Scott Walker
**President, Young America 's Foundation**
* * *

name: Ryan Busse
subject: This Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BUSSE FOR MONTANA • DEMOCRAT • PO BOX 8537 • KALISPELL, MT 59904
body:
This Memorial Day, I want to express my gratitude to our heroes and make a promise to protect the freedoms they fought for.

Ryan Busse puts principles over politics. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/KuMB1syMs0W9O3v5MuCvqQ2?unsubscribedata=nAqVF%2BgnatSRNFALXFYhfZFx32Gb59N2RLLwbNmt%2BuXmulvuh1L4qtkDHiw%2B9aviSuOTOTyKBWvxj9JvY9hCu57fMroHHGKmw1ovr5l1YR9PrjzKNaaddbxVTyd%2Bp6zr4pYzBXoubah%2B05xhTXVdL7v9KyIYLqniMdHTfcB0dqPF5kAAEky0DwTqYOWDwcOHMpOSxQaIHaVIEGawg7yoaF3MtpXbgn8HhoiLnJGUzDZLlAQJ72FQpNxtBOG9QU7Nt6n%2FK3j0doDtrENrUysSgF8G7aIzkPUeY43phscRmcpJjP0NHU0w3mjWS4pm82zfghyv1DdcLYYRa%2BnN0Mq%2BhFhMQBqY2%2Fk34matpO5LRZTan3xk4jHL%2Bo%2FviW7nEg25kr8lnh05pLsunkvumqHXYqI%2BMdQnrsWenMiFVbnSH%2FKAV214x8MyzefUVw3yMt7Q)
---
| | Dear Peter, Today’s Memorial Day is a time to reflect on those who made the ultimate sacrifice to protect and defend the freedoms we hold so dear. Freedoms that, despite the name, are far from free. And in tumultuous times like these, when dangerous radicals attack our rights and freedoms, we must dedicate ourselves to honor them with our own fight. For more than two centuries American heroes have sacrificed to protect what we hold dear. Today we honor them — brave men and women who fought for our democracy, our constitution, and our freedom. “Land of the free and home of the brave” is only possible because of people standing up and fighting when it is time. This Memorial Day, I urge you to take time to reflect on those who gave their life to support our collective American Dream and defend our prosperity, but I also challenge you to honor them with your own fight for our democracy. You may not be called to a battlefield, you may not face life‐threatening attacks or flying bullets. But our state and our democracy are under attack. Please do not squander the sacrifices of our heroes by shirking from responsibility now. We owe it to all who fought and sacrificed before us to stand up and defend what they fought for. Thank you,
Ryan
---
