clean_body: This Memorial Day, I want to express my gratitude to our heroes and make a promise to protect the freedoms they fought for. Ryan Busse puts principles over politics. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Dear Peter, Today’s Memorial Day is a time to reflect on those who made the ultimate sacrifice to protect and defend the freedoms we hold so dear. Freedoms that, despite the name, are far from free. And in tumultuous times like these, when dangerous radicals attack our rights and freedoms, we must dedicate ourselves to honor them with our own fight. For more than two centuries American heroes have sacrificed to protect what we hold dear. Today we honor them — brave men and women who fought for our democracy, our constitution, and our freedom. “Land of the free and home of the brave” is only possible because of people standing up and fighting when it is time. This Memorial Day, I urge you to take time to reflect on those who gave their life to support our collective American Dream and defend our prosperity, but I also challenge you to honor them with your own fight for our democracy. You may not be called to a battlefield, you may not face life‐threatening attacks or flying bullets. But our state and our democracy are under attack. Please do not squander the sacrifices of our heroes by shirking from responsibility now. We owe it to all who fought and sacrificed before us to stand up and defend what they fought for. Thank you, Ryan PAID FOR BY BUSSE FOR MONTANA • DEMOCRAT • PO BOX 8537 • KALISPELL, MT 59904 Busse for Montana PO Box 8537 Kalispell, MT 59904 United States If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: To receive less email from us, . name: Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.
subject: Honoring Fallen Soldiers
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TEAM KENNEDY
body:
On Memorial Day, Americans honor the soldiers that have fallen in war.

|
---
| | | | [**We honor them for having been WILLING to make the ultimate sacrifice.**](https://www.kennedy24.com/donate?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=memorial_day_5_27_24_a1&n=1)

---
|
---
[](https://www.kennedy24.com/donate?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=memorial_day_5_27_24_a1&n=2)
---
[](https://www.kennedy24.com/donate?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=memorial_day_5_27_24_a1&n=3)
[](https://www.kennedy24.com/donate?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=memorial_day_5_27_24_a1&n=4)
---
|
---
Dear Peter,
On Memorial Day, Americans honor the soldiers that have fallen in war. We do not distinguish whether the wars were just or unjust, not on this day, but instead we honor them for having made the ultimate sacrifice.
What we really honor them for is having been WILLING to make the ultimate sacrifice. We admire them not because they died, but because they were willing to lay their lives on the line.
That is the kind of bravery we need today. We desperately need people who believe in our country, who believe in each other, so much that they are willing to put that above themselves.
As President of the United States, my job will be to elevate the conduct of America’s government to the level that deserves that kind of devotion. We will be a force for peace and healing in the world, and a bastion of freedom and justice at home.
That is what I will do in honor of those who sacrificed everything for the country that they loved.
| |
---
---
Sincerely,
Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.

name: Callie Barr
subject: Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Callie Barr for Congress
body:
Today, we’ll be holding many folks in our hearts, honoring the profound sacrifices made by our brave service members and their families.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85713633/475650947/1201310784?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYTI3OGZmZjktMzkxYy1lZjExLTg2ZDAtNjA0NWJkZDllMDk2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hMbr3MiPE3cihAyDjpcqKpzblJUnhdLA1ybH2z_YVlw=&emci=9e78fff9-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=a278fff9-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=9697413)
---
| Peter,

On Memorial Day, we remember our fallen. And in this remembrance, we get a better understanding of what it means to be an American—of who we are.

When Matt left for combat in Iraq, our future was uncertain. We didn’t know if we’d have one wedding anniversary or ten, if we’d ever have kids, if, even if he came home, we’d be the same people we were before he left. In this experience, we readied ourselves for any outcome, embracing what we believed to be something so much bigger than us: our faith and love in one another as Americans.

In the months and years that followed, we lost friends, and we learned to cope and live with the new normal from Matt’s traumatic brain injury and PTSD.

Today, we’ll be holding many folks in our hearts, honoring the profound sacrifices made by our brave service members and their families. Their courage leaves us with an enduring legacy of unwavering American resilience.

While Memorial Day is about the remembrance of those who have passed, we know that we honor that remembrance by seeing each other as Americans first.

We look out for each other rather than leave one another behind.

We memorialize those we’ve lost not just with words but through our actions.

And we fight for our democracy, just as our friends, family, and neighbors fought for it.

**As we gather in remembrance today, let's honor those who gave everything for our freedom by continuing to stand together, support one another, and cherish the democracy they fought so valiantly to protect.**

With deepest gratitude,

Callie
---

* * *

name: Gloria Johnson
subject: May Update: Major moves and moments.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gloria for Tennessee
body:
What a month it's been, y'all!

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85713655/475650975/-849459103?refcode=ea.972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NzIxOTNkMi0zOTFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6788Gs16D3QayImtOh8RwIek4mD15-bf6UXrKlzZ6EI=&emci=d49695b3-171a-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=46764483)
---
Hey, y’all! With the Tennessee state legislature officially out of session, it's full steam ahead for our campaign. We're now less than six months from one of the most critical elections we've faced, and there's no slowing down. Here’s what’s been happening 👇
* * *
📺 A National Spotlight 📺 I had the privilege of joining Ali Velshi and State Rep. Justin Jones on MSNBC to discuss the troubling bill in Tennessee that allows teachers to carry guns in schools. I made it clear that we need sensible gun control, not more guns in our classrooms. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85713656/475650976/-803653937?v=3bf1Fs8ZDQw&refcode=ea.972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NzIxOTNkMi0zOTFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6788Gs16D3QayImtOh8RwIek4mD15-bf6UXrKlzZ6EI=&emci=d49695b3-171a-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=46764483) In 2023, the U.S. averaged nearly one school shooting incident per day. One of those shootings was right here in Nashville. On March 27, 2023, gun violence tore through Covenant School and devastated our community. Survivors and families begged their elected leaders for change. GOP politicians silenced them. Now, instead of doing the bare minimum — passing the commonsense reforms that work like background checks and red flag laws — they want to arm our teachers. Giving teachers guns solves nothing. This is just one of the many issues where our state needs real change, not reactionary policies. **[You can watch my full reaction here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85713657/475650977/-803653937?v=3bf1Fs8ZDQw&refcode=ea.972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NzIxOTNkMi0zOTFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6788Gs16D3QayImtOh8RwIek4mD15-bf6UXrKlzZ6EI=&emci=d49695b3-171a-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=46764483)**
* * *
🎊 New Campaign Office in Knoxville 🎊 We've officially opened our new campaign HQ in Knoxville! This new hub is where we'll coordinate our efforts to reach every Tennessean and where volunteers can gather to help us spread our message of hope and change. It’s a big step forward in building a campaign that truly represents the people. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85713658/475650978/209297649?refcode=ea.972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ms=ea.972193d2-391c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MDYxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NzIxOTNkMi0zOTFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6788Gs16D3QayImtOh8RwIek4mD15-bf6UXrKlzZ6EI=)
* * *
🚌 95 County Bus Tour 🚌 Our 95 County Bus Tour is in full swing! clean_body: What a month it's been, y'all! | Hey, y’all! With the Tennessee state legislature officially out of session, it's full steam ahead for our campaign. We're now less than six months from one of the most critical elections we've faced, and there's no slowing down. Here’s what’s been happening 👇 📺 A National Spotlight 📺 I had the privilege of joining Ali Velshi and State Rep. Justin Jones on MSNBC to discuss the troubling bill in Tennessee that allows teachers to carry guns in schools. I made it clear that we need sensible gun control, not more guns in our classrooms. In 2023, the U.S. averaged nearly one school shooting incident per day. One of those shootings was right here in Nashville. On March 27, 2023, gun violence tore through Covenant School and devastated our community. Survivors and families begged their elected leaders for change. GOP politicians silenced them. Now, instead of doing the bare minimum — passing the commonsense reforms that work like background checks and red flag laws — they want to arm our teachers. Giving teachers guns solves nothing. This is just one of the many issues where our state needs real change, not reactionary policies. 🎊 New Campaign Office in Knoxville 🎊 We've officially opened our new campaign HQ in Knoxville! This new hub is where we'll coordinate our efforts to reach every Tennessean and where volunteers can gather to help us spread our message of hope and change. It’s a big step forward in building a campaign that truly represents the people. = 🚌 95 County Bus Tour 🚌 Our 95 County Bus Tour is in full swing! = = On Friday, we visited OUT Memphis to see the amazing work being done for LGBTQ+ youth in crisis. = And then went on to Ounce of Hope Aquaponics Cannabis Farm and Dispensary. It’s past time for us to cast aside the failed War on Drugs, and the devastating and disproportionate effects that it’s had on minority communities & low-income folks. = We’ve been traveling from one end of Tennessee to the other, engaging with voters, discussing your concerns, and sharing how we plan to address them in Washington. This tour will help us understand the diverse needs of our state and ensure everyone's voice is heard. Mental Health Awareness Month May is Mental Health Awareness Month, a crucial time to remember that mental health is just as important as physical health. In the state legislature, I’ve pushed for better mental health resources for adolescents and I promise to continue that work in Washington. Remember, if you or someone you know is struggling, the 988 suicide and crisis hotline is available 24/7. Memorial Day Reflections Today, as we mark Memorial Day, I invite you to join me in reflecting on the sacrifices made by our servicemen and women across our nation’s history. Their bravery and dedication are the bedrock upon which we build our efforts for a better tomorrow. Let’s hold them in our hearts, not just today, but every day, as we continue to fight for the values they defended with their lives. That’s just a bit of what I’ve been up to in May, y’all! It’s important to me to keep you up to date on news from the campaign trail and beyond, so you’ll see more news from me in the coming months. If you want the latest news in real-time, be sure to follow me on social media: | All my best, Gloria Johnson = | = Gloria Johnson is running for the U.S. Senate because Tennesseans deserve leaders who care. For too long, elected officials like Marsha Blackburn have done absolutely nothing to make Tennesseans’ lives better. The only interests they’ve cared about are special interests. The only people they’ve championed are their donors. Gloria will advocate for real solutions to protect our communities from gun violence, make health care more affordable and accessible for all of us, secure access to the ballot box, and defend reproductive rights. | = = Follow along: | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Gloria for Tennessee Contributions or gifts to Gloria for Tennessee are not tax deductible. name: Catherine Engelbrecht
subject: Have a Meaningful Memorial Day

name: Ty Pinkins
subject: Honoring our veterans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ty Pinkins for Congress
body:
I’ve experienced firsthand the sacrifice required to serve our country

Peter,

Today, we honor the courageous men and women who have served our country with unwavering bravery, commitment, and selflessness.

As I reflect on the meaning of today, I am reminded of the courage it takes to serve, the sacrifices made, and the unwavering commitment to duty. Their bravery has paved the way for a safer, freer world, and for that, we owe them an immeasurable debt of gratitude.

I have personally experienced the profound impact that service can have on a family. In my own family, there has been no exception.

Before I enlisted in the military, I remember my father being in the peak of health – robust and lively. He actively participated in our community, even playing center field for our local softball team. This was the image I carried with me when I left, but he was not the same person I returned home to. I was taken aback to find my father had lost his sight, had undergone double amputation, and was now reliant on a wheelchair. Both my parents were also battling kidney failure. Witnessing my loved ones in such a condition was truly heartbreaking.

Experiences such as these have profoundly shaped my commitment to advocating for veterans’ rights and ensuring that our heroes receive the care and support they deserve.

Today, we remember not only their service but also the unwavering support of their families and loved ones. They have stood by them, providing strength, encouragement, and hope through the most challenging times. Their sacrifices are often unrecognized, but they are integral to the success and well-being of our veteran community.

As a fellow veteran and as your candidate for United States Senate, I extend my deepest thanks to each and every one of you. Your service and sacrifice will never be forgotten. Together, we honor your legacy and strive to build a future worthy of your sacrifices.

As we commemorate Memorial Day, let us also reaffirm our commitment to each other. Let us stand united, as we did in service, to support and uplift our veteran community. Together, we can build a future that honors your sacrifices and provides the opportunities, care, and support you so rightfully deserve.

Thank you for your service, your sacrifice, and your unwavering dedication to our country. It is an honor to stand with you, and I am proud to be your advocate in this journey. May God bless you all, and may God bless the United States of America.

With gratitude,

_Ty Pinkins_

* * *
| The son of a tractor driver, Ty doesn’t come from a wealthy family. He was born and raised in the small town of Rolling Fork, and is a proud son of the Mississippi Delta.

Frustrated by the lack of action from lawmakers on the issues facing people in Mississippi–and believing that the hard-working voters of Mississippi deserve much better–Ty Pinkins decided to run for office: the United States Senate in 2024.

---

name: Christina Bohannan
subject: A day to honor and remember
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christina Bohannan for Congress
body:
Memorial Day is a time for reflection, gratitude, and remembrance.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85714273/475653141/182211867?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI2NjBhYy0zYTFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Thbmwuw-8LC8BtswtZ5CJhU-iuYdeIYs9RWjTIBmVoo=)
Peter,

This Memorial Day, I want to take a moment to honor and remember the brave men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice for our country. Their courage and dedication have secured the freedoms we cherish today.

As we commemorate this day, I'm reminded of the Keokuk Road of Honor, the road leading to Iowa’s only national cemetery. For years, I have been calling attention to the need for its reconstruction to ensure that we properly honor our fallen heroes. I am heartened to see that reconstruction has finally begun, a step forward in ensuring that the journey to pay respects is befitting of their sacrifice.
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85714275/475653143/212548770?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjIyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjI2NjBhYy0zYTFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Thbmwuw-8LC8BtswtZ5CJhU-iuYdeIYs9RWjTIBmVoo=&emci=ef54ddc8-1e19-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=3b2660ac-3a1c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=13891890)
Memorial Day is a time for reflection, gratitude, and remembrance. Let us come together to honor the legacy of those who have served and remember the families who have lost loved ones in service to our nation.

– Christina Bohannan

**BREAKING NEWS: NextGen PAC is endorsing these incumbents for reelection to Congress in 2024:**
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/85714525/475653901/1050946684?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS85OTMyMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMGRhMWQ1Zi0zYjFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=0t5_kQnv7T7ifpspBY6OEGNR9KXzjqdgc4XDH2W2_fg=&emci=694f3d33-361c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=20da1d5f-3b1c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=6787172)
[Chip in now to defend democracy in 2024 and beyond!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/85714526/475653902/1050946684?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS85OTMyMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMGRhMWQ1Zi0zYjFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=0t5_kQnv7T7ifpspBY6OEGNR9KXzjqdgc4XDH2W2_fg=&emci=694f3d33-361c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=20da1d5f-3b1c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=6787172)
All of these candidates are running to make progress a reality in 2024 and beyond. They’ve proven again and again that their first priority is the people they represent. Instead of bowing to corporate interests, they’ve stood up for our democracy and empowered young voters to make a difference.
_We deserve leaders like this._
NextGen PAC is proud to endorse:
* **Rep. Steven Horsford** (NV-04), **Rep. Dina Titus** (NV-01), and Rep. **Susie Lee** (NV-03) for Nevada;
* **Rep. Joaquin Castro** (TX-20), **Rep. Greg Casar** (TX-35), **Rep. Jasmine Crockett** (TX-30), and **Rep. Vicente Gonzalez Jr.** (TX-34) for Texas;
* **Rep. Rashida Tlaib** (MI-12) and **Rep. Hillary Scholten** (MI-03) for Michigan;
* **Rep. Mary Gay Scanlon** (PA-05), **Rep. Dwight Evans** (PA-03), **Rep. Chris Deluzio** (PA-17), **Rep. Chrissy Houlahan**(PA-06), **Rep. Madeleine Dean** (PA-04), **Rep. Brendan Boyle** (PA-02), **Rep. Summer Lee** (PA-12), **Rep. Susan Wild** (PA-07), and **Rep. Matt Cartwright** (PA-08) for Pennsylvania;
* **Rep. Chris Pappas** (NH-01) for New Hampshire; and
* **Rep. Don Davis** (NC-01) for North Carolina.

The stakes are high. But defending our democracy starts with us electing candidates committed to pushing us forward, not holding us back.
Young Americans have proven time and time again that when they get to the polls, they make all the difference. We want to make sure they’re ready to show up.
So we have to ask: [Will you chip in $5, or anything you can, to help us uphold our democracy and fight for progress?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/85714527/475653903/1050946684?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNTkcvMS85OTMyMCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMGRhMWQ1Zi0zYjFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=0t5_kQnv7T7ifpspBY6OEGNR9KXzjqdgc4XDH2W2_fg=&emci=694f3d33-361c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=20da1d5f-3b1c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=6787172)

clean_body: | BREAKING NEWS: NextGen PAC is endorsing these incumbents for reelection to Congress in 2024: All of these candidates are running to make progress a reality in 2024 and beyond. They’ve proven again and again that their first priority is the people they represent. Instead of bowing to corporate interests, they’ve stood up for our democracy and empowered young voters to make a difference. We deserve leaders like this. NextGen PAC is proud to endorse: Rep. Steven Horsford (NV-04), Rep. Dina Titus (NV-01), and Rep. Susie Lee (NV-03) for Nevada; Rep. Joaquin Castro (TX-20), Rep. Greg Casar (TX-35), Rep. Jasmine Crockett (TX-30), and Rep. Vicente Gonzalez Jr. (TX-34) for Texas; Rep. Rashida Tlaib (MI-12) and Rep. Hillary Scholten (MI-03) for Michigan; Rep. Mary Gay Scanlon (PA-05), Rep. Dwight Evans (PA-03), Rep. Chris Deluzio (PA-17), Rep. Chrissy Houlahan(PA-06), Rep. Madeleine Dean (PA-04), Rep. Brendan Boyle (PA-02), Rep. Summer Lee (PA-12), Rep. Susan Wild (PA-07), and Rep. Matt Cartwright (PA-08) for Pennsylvania; Rep. Chris Pappas (NH-01) for New Hampshire; and Rep. Don Davis (NC-01) for North Carolina. The stakes are high. But defending our democracy starts with us electing candidates committed to pushing us forward, not holding us back. Young Americans have proven time and time again that when they get to the polls, they make all the difference. We want to make sure they’re ready to show up. So we have to ask: | | Your support makes all the difference, NextGen PAC | Paid for by NextGen Climate Action Committee; ; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. | = |

name: Wesley Bell
subject: Celebrating the fallen, and honoring the promises made to those who are still with us.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
Memorial Day is not just a day off but a time to reflect on the true cost of freedom. It’s a day to pay tribute to our fallen heroes and express our gratitude for their service – _**their courage and dedication has secured many of the freedoms we cherish today.**_

As we gather with family and friends this Memorial Day, let us take a moment to remember and honor the brave men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice for our country.

In Congress, I’ll always vote to support the families they left behind and stand with the veterans who are still with us, _who have given so much._ Their service and sacrifice should never be forgotten. Today, and everyday, I hope you’ll join me in honoring our heroes.

**Wishing you a safe and peaceful Memorial Day.**

Thank you,

Wesley

| PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: My first memory of my oldest brother
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
Growing up with brothers in the military gave Memorial Day a special meaning

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2192%2E4032634%2El9vdyf)
---
My first memory of my oldest brother is from the morning he left to join the Air Force. Don Reed was nineteen, I was three. The memory is like a five-second home-movie fragment that plays in my head. It’s 1953. We’ve driven to Oklahoma City, where he will report for duty. My mother and I are standing close together on a sidewalk, her full cotton skirt blowing around me. She’s crying. He’s halfway up some steps when he turns. Squinting in the blisteringly bright Oklahoma sun, he waves and gives us a quick wink. Then the memory is over. Six days a week, my mother rushed to the mailbox to see if there was a letter from Don Reed — or later on, from my brother John or my brother David, after each of them went off to the military. When a letter came from one of the boys, the day was extra-sunny. She would read it and re-read it, and call my aunts or my grandmother to read it again over the phone. My brothers all made it home safely, and for that, I’m eternally grateful. Growing up with brothers in the military gave Memorial Day a special meaning: Understanding what others have lost and honoring even more the sacrifice of those who died in service to our country and those who love them. We owe them a debt that can never be repaid. In Washington, I’ll keep pushing our government to make decisions in the best interest of our service members, their families, and our national security — to take care of our veterans when they come home and making sure they have access to affordable housing and healthcare. No matter how you honor Memorial Day, I hope you have a meaningful day. Elizabeth

---

name: Bonnie Watson Coleman
subject: On Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bonnie Watson Coleman for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85716622/475660297/1328194270?source=EM_NBI_BWC_20240527_ST_MemorialDay_0_3MO_Bonnie_NA_NA_MemorialDay&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ODc2MjhlMi0zZTFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Z4kExupz-Jl1p0WWRms0Qm-VTpy1k6ykF3PFXFkzYBI=&emci=6e96d5db-3e1c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=687628e2-3e1c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=30096098) | I hope you will take some time today, on Memorial Day, to pause and reflect on the sacrifices made by our service members.

Men and women who answered the call of duty and gave their lives to protect the liberties we enjoy today. Too frequently in Washington, we talk about our hard-fought and hard-won freedoms without truly thinking of the sacrifices made to attain them.

Let us never forget the sons and daughters of New Jersey who put everything on the line to ensure we can speak freely, worship freely, vote without fear, love and marry freely, and provide our children and grandchildren with a better tomorrow.

Today, let’s remember that equality, democracy, and opportunity are worth fighting for. Let’s honor the memories of those who lost their lives in the pursuit of those freedoms. And let’s take solace in the fact that we don’t stand alone.

In this together,

Bonnie

---
* * *

---
Bonnie Watson Coleman represents New Jersey's 12th district in Congress. She is a strong fighter for progressive values and has dedicated herself to the service of her community.

name: Jesse James Mullen
subject: Why this day exists
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mullen for Secretary of State
body:
Friends, Today, we gather across our state and country to celebrate Memorial Day. I’m taking a moment to extend my heartfelt wishes to you and yours on this day of reflection and gratitude, as we honor the brave men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our country. | | | [](http://links.mullenformontana.com/u/click?_t=96962950e68f455895e83c6431fce0d7&_m=2f4f8e4f242140f7a2e8d657644acec3&_e=JyxLqQnDzZ1VSpHGJricn2kowBM-o1vADNCsGH2_xWAZWjhnE0DlW2AiL7Lc4ZTlCku2LBzBP9JlqRPWx-H2DYZzp00PlWuZxXhalK1yQpy1DxXIhtuup3EV5m0G7af9c75pxYX3DL65mZmF7IKU3MhgqmuW3L1hg-pZn4MJklQZ-sbxk721dX6SNdz2cY2dD1IF--KmIRb6VTQr9nKoGAEIGPrF8v6G7pFNXzbZ84-PTaUaHaLaiZUgSOBRFl539jNcPuqKycrDopW5eGdyQQw5BNQrS1EliXA1ILKpCdAR-3nzxSrvdBI1CW2SZES0n9X_vZAgSDXJuuExTv1oasnbPuoDFtU8XLZcrGRBhHnRXlVfZq6ULQB_ZSiFQI-1DN7ITCkWHEvYwSCckYSyYQ%3D%3D) Friends, Today, we gather across our state and country to celebrate Memorial Day. I’m taking a moment to extend my heartfelt wishes to you and yours on this day of reflection and gratitude, as we honor the brave men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our country. The courage and dedication of these heroes protected the freedoms and values we hold dear. It is our responsibility to honor their legacy. In a state with a strong history of military service, it’s important that we take today to come together to pay tribute to these heroes and support their families who gave so much. While we enjoy the day with family and friends, let’s not forget those who made enjoying this day even possible in the first place. May you have a peaceful Memorial Day. Sincerely,
Jesse James Mullen
Democratic Candidate for Secretary of State [](http://links.mullenformontana.com/u/click?_t=96962950e68f455895e83c6431fce0d7&_m=2f4f8e4f242140f7a2e8d657644acec3&_e=JyxLqQnDzZ1VSpHGJricn2kowBM-o1vADNCsGH2_xWAZWjhnE0DlW2AiL7Lc4ZTlCku2LBzBP9JlqRPWx-H2DYZzp00PlWuZxXhalK1yQpy1DxXIhtuup3EV5m0G7af9c75pxYX3DL65mZmF7IKU3MhgqmuW3L1hg-pZn4MJklQZ-sbxk721dX6SNdz2cY2dD1IF--KmIRb6VTQr9nKoGAEIGPrF8v6G7pFNXzbZ84-PTaUaHaLaiZUgSOBRFl53dIk66fDsq30Yxoqu9-tu4zFvE-EYp5zeAE0FxcT1ZlFQjZowIEb-wmOvGsJhjbk35eDfKQg5Na1Z4OoMwYUIT2CJVX0Bcdw452jFq3Ot4ohOu3Ak3xOjkd1RvJUDACoz2JzwmwCc1jv43iqW1UqYPQ%3D%3D) Montana needs your help to defeat the Far Right and protect our values of caring, community, and free and fair Elections. name: Salud Carbajal
subject: Celebrating Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Salud Carbajal for Congress
body:
This Memorial Day, please join me in remembering the sacrifices so many Americans made for our freedoms.

Our country is what it is today because of those who lost their lives in service. We are grateful to those who paid the ultimate price and will never forget the sacrifice they made for us.

I am firmly committed to fighting for our servicemembers and veterans to ensure they and their families receive the benefits they deserve. Thank you for standing with me as we acknowledge these brave men and women today.

Sincerely,
Salud Carbajal

Paid for by Salud Carbajal for Congress
PO Box 1290, Santa Barbara, CA 93102
info@saludcarbajal.com; www.saludcarbajal.com

name: Bob Lorinser
subject: 🇺🇸 What they died for
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF DR
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP12/1/92793/images/Untitled%20design.png]
What They were Fighting For
Friends,
On Memorial Day, we honor the men and women who died while serving in the United States Armed Forces.
They were protecting several core principles and values that are fundamental to the American way of life.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP12/1/92793/images/memorial%20day.jpeg]
*
They
defended
our
free
speech
and
civil
liberties.
*
They
protected
and
defended
us
from
hostile
nations,
terrorist
organizations,
and
other
threats.
*
They
promoted
global
peace
and
stability.
*
They
supported
and
strengthened
us
in
maintaining
a
stable
and
cooperative
global
order.
*
They
upheld
values
such
as
justice
and
equality
both
domestically
and
internationally.
Today, we pause to reflect on the indomitable courage and unwavering dedication of these fallen heroes.
Their service, marked by bravery and sacrifice, has left an indelible mark on our nation.
We honor them not just for their service, but for the principles for which they fought, reminding us of the values we hold dear.
-Bob
Bob Lorinser
Candidate for US Congress
Northern Michigan / Upper Peninsula
Democrat, MI01
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP12/1/92793/images/dbsignature.png]

Friends of Dr. Bob
PO Box 936
Marquette, MI 49855
United States

clean_body: [] What They were Fighting For Friends, On Memorial Day, we honor the men and women who died while serving in the United States Armed Forces. They were protecting several core principles and values that are fundamental to the American way of life. [] They defended our free speech and civil liberties. They protected and defended us from hostile nations, terrorist organizations, and other threats. They promoted global peace and stability. They supported and strengthened us in maintaining a stable and cooperative global order. * They upheld values such as justice and equality both domestically and internationally. Today, we pause to reflect on the indomitable courage and unwavering dedication of these fallen heroes. Their service, marked by bravery and sacrifice, has left an indelible mark on our nation. We honor them not just for their service, but for the principles for which they fought, reminding us of the values we hold dear. -Bob Bob Lorinser Candidate for US Congress Northern Michigan / Upper Peninsula Democrat, MI01 [] Or, send a check to Friends of Dr. Bob PO Box 936 Marquette, MI 49855 United States PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF DR. BOB

subject: Honoring our fallen
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Padora for Colorado
body:
Hey folks, it’s John. In honor of Memorial Day, I wanted to reach out to reflect on the brave men and women who service our country in uniform.
__ | | | [](http://links.padoraforcongress.com/u/click?_t=1632d2fd715245289db23a5b99e588cb&_m=08148f0efe10493b9cb72c84fa0a531d&_e=RDfDPygG04CBUnCiU85Zl0StQUciQFtaELtLfclxDiay1jQls9BmiPMiyRQFVnG7kSVtGNKfErihWKFbP5knw_MnjjdtuUios8YmMCz1THZjLyxPcHiTYORuwXq_9d8Q1PGDZzwVq2uYdfYkyM7iO3bdvjNVf-YcwtX5s0xiq8cml3YnG-c2-3z8R0P_IbLLOqZgS4rvPSo-jj47KE1eP0GBkThzHzs7BItpT_3TKtLS6UkbQ07IIJCuXPiWjJEHMUb6bgQivue8bvvsrpNbVkWpWG4ARi9Fb1E2aQuABq4nwckbartu0K7UJN-JY8n9jnJudh1FChHZNBQSxTHBx6J9MA5ii0nlxjPMgPIrYdZm8BpZg3ESuWUsclj2Hk1f4NONFnH4x63fuF46N4Or57KVN9irI_p-mXyIqJAJTXI%3D) Hey folks, it’s John. In honor of Memorial Day, I wanted to reach out to reflect on the brave men and women who service our country in uniform. Today, I’m thinking of all the courageous service members and their families who have made the ultimate sacrifice for our nation. Colorado specifically has made enormous contributions to the defense of freedom in this country and I want to honor every one of our fallen Colorado heroes. This weekend is to honor and remember all those who laid down their lives in service of our great nation and the families who lost their loved ones. Sincerely, John Padora I'm John Padora, a Democrat running for Congress in CD-04. Lauren Boebert recently jumped into my district in a desperate attempt to stay in DC. My campaign is powered by grassroots donations - donate anything you can monthly to keep Boebert OUT of this seat. name: Greg Landsman
subject: Happy Memorial Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Landsman for Congress](https://click
body:
To all of those who have answered the call to serve our country: Thank you for your service.

| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85719307/475666854/-1268629450?refcode=em_msg_2024.05.27_b1_gl_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDg5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MDYyZWE1OC00MjFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BH4xKapJuTC8-brfN4ohUNJdwzMAm-VLtcaK3BTztgk=&emci=68ba99b8-291c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=9062ea58-421c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=10283101)
---
Friend,

**As our nation celebrates Memorial Day today, I wanted to take a quick moment to honor our veterans and men and women in uniform.**

Our troops exemplify the kind of brave and selfless leadership that has made our country great.

It’s a humbling experience to serve the people of Ohio, knowing that it's our men and women in uniform who have kept America’s experiment in self-government alive over the centuries.

As the representative for Ohio’s First District, I will always fight to ensure our troops and veterans receive the support and care they deserve after sacrificing so much for our country.

**So to all of those who have answered the call to serve our country, thank you for your service.** I wish you and your loved ones a restful Memorial Day weekend! In gratitude, _Greg_

name: Tim Walz
subject: When you see the flags at half-staff today
disclaimer_text: paid for by Tim Walz for Governor
body:
Peter, Memorial Day is a day to reflect on the freedoms we are so fortunate to have in this country and to recognize the extraordinary costs of attaining those freedoms.
|
---
Peter,
Memorial Day is a day to reflect on the freedoms we are so fortunate to have in this country and to recognize the extraordinary costs of attaining those freedoms.
When you see the flags at half-staff today, please take a moment to reflect on and honor the spirit, bravery, and perseverance of the generations of Armed Forces members and their families who made the ultimate sacrifice.
As we strive and work for world peace, let us hold all who have served on our behalf close to our hearts.
I’m grateful,
Tim Walz

** **
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Governor Walz and Lt. Governor Flanagan have overcome unprecedented challenges to provide historic investments in education, create new, good-paying jobs, and increase access to health care. And with your help, they can defeat their extremist Republican opponents and continue to move our state forward, as one Minnesota. ## SEX, MARRIAGE + DIVORCE According To Jesus
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5518.1.aa065a70463b57d4ac47deb41ccce6ae)
---
What are grounds for a **biblical divorce**? This question has been hotly debated for 2000 years in the Church. **Jesus** says grounds for a biblical divorce = "_except for sexual immorality_." What does that mean? Is _abuse_ grounds for a biblical divorce? I address this and more in the series we are going through at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5519.1.11e51bffbac921ab457aae4a18eabb4c) over the[Gospel of Matthew](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5520.1.9961bfc7fa49a98e3f6c39e916728aa9). I also address how to _prevent_ a marriage from ever reaching the place of grounds for a biblical divorce. Watch [Matthew Pt. 9 - SEX, MARRIAGE + DIVORCE](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5521.1.2b3f18bfdff668b2a68baedf3f6aaf2f) here: [https://youtube.com/live/QBndJugRbTo](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5522.1.968521a18c29fc45ec0f154e07f99fb6) Thank you,
Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer

## Special Guest Speaker Coming To Sheridan.Church
---
|
---
Over the last several years, we have been blessed at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5524.1.fcada3fd60bd91b6c61cea0627b5a506) with some tremendous guest speakers such as [Rabbi Jonathan Cahn](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5525.1.b57020334c6ce5330571dfb1199c172b), [Lara Trump](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5526.1.7f9de7389abd6a5503be6444c1840cf2), Perry Stone, [Darryl Strawberry](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5527.1.ec5f1ac38fba818e5469abfb8d355fa5), [Mayor Rudy Giuliani](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5528.1.df830ea898f1c7d3991a943bec9262a3), [Roger Stone](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5529.1.fcf101b6d232c073a5d161ba4f0929ed), Rodney Howard-Browne and so many others. In the future we have [General Flynn](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5530.1.2a5c709b4c5aef6df3c07b3df38105db), [Tim Tebow](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5531.1.f1044666115172f3ea4e6f6775fffdd5) and [Riley Gaines](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5532.1.482a3d792ae77a7083fb685dffb37a29) coming to share. But one guest that I am super excited to share with you who is coming to [Sheridan](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5533.1.4180bed3924719eb339528aa8f7ec959) is a former professor of mine from **Oral Roberts University**. **Dr. John Swails** is the director of ORU's _Center for Israel and Middle East Studies_. **Dr. Swails** earned an A.B., M.A., and Ph.D. in history from the University of Georgia as well as an M.A. in near east studies from Brandeis University. He has been nominated as a _Who's Who_ among American Teachers for multiple years, I learned so much by sitting in many of **Dr. Swails** lectures that I know this service will bless you. Join me on **Wednesday, June 12 at 7pm** for a [LIVE Q&A w/Dr. John Swails](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5534.1.af82dc8aa52406bd90e0857289f63bdc). **Address** : 7901 E 41st St Tulsa, OK 74145 For more information visit: [www.sheridan.church/events](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5535.1.e7e0fa440456ee0e511185674f8e93fa) Thank you,
Pastor Jackson Lahmeyer
---
---
| [ ](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5536.1.784f0cca4b350a05b167f3fa95d9a932)

One of my goals at [Sheridan.Church](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5537.1.5be03fc885759fc6e4e0558c97452172) is to bring in speakers that are addressing issues we face in our nation. We have been blessed to host: [Lara Trump](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5538.1.c5077d2a34b36634fb78a298ed88a5d8), **Perry Stone** , [Rabbi Jonathan Cahn](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5539.1.e180c11815cf810ea016ea6810655b58), **Rodney Howard-Browne** , [Darry Strawberry](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5540.1.f628e51562cfd9a7508ab21f2c9293da), [Mayor Rudy Giuliani](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5541.1.21658b056177e843cc2b8f44d0e0b8cf), [Roger Stone](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5542.1.64a781f55412b5a85c088e84ac78cc8b), **David Barton** and others in the past. I am excited to announce that [Riley Gaines](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5961?id=10951078.5543.1.703e8c9ed543f31f5505b117dd5f6a4a) will be joining us on Friday, July 26 to address one of the most crucial battles we have right now that centers upon _gender_. name: Rockriver Development
subject: Memorial Day
body:
Honoring the bravest among us...
---
| | | | | |
---
| |
# Memorial Day
---
---
This Memorial Day, let us take a moment to never forget the sacrifices made by our heroes in defense of our country and for a better world. Their selflessness and sacrifice serve as a beacon of inspiration for us all, reminding us of the importance of reflection, gratitude, and the enduring values that define us as a nation. _**"Our obligations to our country never cease but with our lives." John Adams 1808**_ Wishing you a meaningful and respectful Memorial Day. Marc Flores

Team — Earlier this year, the Senate passed the Radiation Exposure Compensation Reauthorization Act (RECA) which renews funding for residents of radiation-exposed communities, including the Navajo Nation right here in Arizona.
Between World War II and the Cold War, the lands of underserved communities like Navajo Nation and the Pueblo Nation in New Mexico were used for uranium mining and atomic weapons testing — leaving lasting harm on the communities and people that live there. Since 1990, RECA has provided financial support to the communities who were exposed to the toxic radiation from mining and testing.
**[But without a House vote, this support is set to run out, leaving victims of exposure at risk. Will you add your name right now to support the renewal of RECA?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85722221/475673826/-1925968684?refcode=AU_AZD_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20240527_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNGUyYjdkMDQtNDYxYy1lZjExLTg2ZDAtNjA0NWJkZDllMDk2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Iqp6P2Q2OgfVmPqTOmlmbOeTJjKeuabO9u68NbV8Vo4=&emci=20a09dfd-451c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=4e2b7d04-461c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=9307116)**

body:
| | | Hi team, it's Diana, the Research Director here at the DSCC.

I'm reaching out because even after multiple indictments and pushing our democracy to the brink, Donald Trump is _still_ statistically tied with Joe Biden in the polls!

We need to know that Democrats everywhere are prepared to stand against Donald Trump -- because it's going to take _all of us_ to keep him from inflicting four more years of misery on the country.

**We've launched our Official 2024 Straw Poll to make sure our grassroots team is prepared to take on Donald Trump once again, but I just checked our records and didn't see your response --_so please answer immediately:_**

[](https://go.dscc.org/go/18092?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20240527-Diana-DianaOfficialStrawPoll23RF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20240527-Diana-DianaOfficialStrawPoll23RF-Pros-sl1&akid=14908%2E9716520%2Epc17LI)

name: Roger Williams
subject: Honoring Our Heroes This Memorial Day
body:
🇺🇲🇺🇲🇺🇲

| | | | [ ](https://links.rogerwilliamsforcongress.com/els/v2/g8-YQr8Ym6H6/Zk8zU1UvclFUeVd1UFJ2c0N5U2J2Qlg2dm5SMkYvVUV6QmU5engrYTRlSXBEcGNvM2o3RXR5QTlCNnBJR0dVNUxCRG9KMEVjcGEwVExHYW1YOHFlRkRxVC9NWVFBeThsMTJTM01UYnVydGc9S0/)
---
---
| | Peter, As we gather with family and friends this Memorial Day, I want to take a moment to reflect on the true meaning of this important day. It is a time to honor and remember the brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our nation. Memorial Day is more than just a long weekend; it is a solemn reminder of the courage, dedication, and selflessness of our fallen heroes. These individuals answered the call to defend our freedoms and protect our way of life, often in the face of unimaginable danger. Their sacrifice is a debt we can never fully repay, but we can and must honor their memory. Thank you to all who have served and to those who continue to serve. Your dedication and bravery are the foundation of our freedom. May God bless you and your families, and may He continue to bless the United States of America. Sincerely,
---
| | [ ](https://links.rogerwilliamsforcongress.com/els/v2/4r_KH4wXxvQ8/Zk8zU1UvclFUeVd1UFJ2c0N5U2J2Qlg2dm5SMkYvVUV6QmU5engrYTRlSXBEcGNvM2o3RXR5QTlCNnBJR0dVNUxCRG9KMEVjcGEwVExHYW1YOHFlRkRxVC9NWVFBeThsMTJTM01UYnVydGc9S0/)
---
| | Roger Williams TX-25
---
|
* * *
---
| |
---
| | Roger Williams is a Christian, conservative, pro-life, and second amendment supporter taking Texas values to Washington, D.C. He is a small business owner with over 45 years of experience and is running for Congress to create economic growth, jobs, and provide strong leadership. Roger worked alongside President Trump to fight against high taxes, rising energy prices, and soaring healthcare costs. His passion for conservative leadership led him to support Republican candidates from the White House to the county courthouse. Roger serves on the Board of Trustees for the Bush School of Government and Public Service at Texas A&M University, the Texas Christian University Board of Trustees, and the National Football Foundation and College Football Hall of Fame Board of Directors.

body:
In Congress, I am continuing the work to ensure veterans have access to the healthcare, economic, and community support they deserve.
# [](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/892?refcode=em20240527-3919-footer&t=1&akid=3919%2E420207%2ET4i0rQ)
Happy Memorial Day, Peter!
Today we wanted to take a moment to remember and honor those who have made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our nation.
In Congress, Pramila is continuing the work to ensure veterans have access to the health care, economic assistance, and community support that they deserve. But there’s so much more work to be done to ensure those who have put their lives on the line for our freedoms have the support and opportunities they need to succeed.
**On this Memorial Day, we must reaffirm our movement’s commitment to ensuring that veterans and their families have the opportunity to thrive, not just survive in America.**[**If you stand with us, please chip in $3 or more today to support our efforts.**](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20240527.ZMg1_n&refcode=em20240527-3919&t=2&akid=3919%2E420207%2ET4i0rQ)
**[Donate ››](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/47393?c0=pj-email-20240527.ZMg1_n&refcode=em20240527-3919&t=21&akid=3919%2E420207%2ET4i0rQ)**
In solidarity,
Jayapal HQ
**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20240527-3919-footer&t=22&refcode2=3919_420207_T4i0rQ&akid=3919%2E420207%2ET4i0rQ)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=23&akid=3919%2E420207%2ET4i0rQ)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=3919%2E420207%2ET4i0rQ)

name: Gwen Moore
subject: 27,316 flags
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Gwen Moore for Congress
body:
Today we give our hearts and minds to remember the brave souls who paid the ultimate price to protect our nation, allowing us the freedom to walk towards a brighter future. Their sacrifices are not just a memory but a beacon of hope and resilience that guide us forward.

Here in Wisconsin, 27,316 flags proudly fly to represent those in our state who died serving our country over the centuries. Because of our heroes’ sacrifices, we believe in the possibility of a better future.

Let’s honor them with the legacy we all will write together.

Together, #WeRemember, #WeRepresent, and #WeSoar!

Gwen
Proudly representing Wisconsin's 4th Congressional District
Proud Co-Leader of the Violence Against Women Act
100% Pro-Choice
100% Democracy Defender

About Gwen: The first African-American woman in Wisconsin history to serve in the U.S. House of Representatives, Congresswoman Gwen Moore, took her official oath of office on January 4, 2005. She served in the Wisconsin State Senate from 1993-2004. Before her election to the Senate, Congresswoman Moore served two consecutive terms in the Wisconsin State Assembly from 1989-92.

name: Mike Thompson
subject: On this Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://support
body:
# [#]
Peter,

Today is Memorial Day - a day to honor the brave soldiers who died in service to our country and celebrate the freedoms they fought to protect.

Throughout our history, hundreds of thousands of Americans made the ultimate sacrifice as they served us with dignity and pride. Every day, many continue to put on their uniforms and risk their lives to protect our home.

This Memorial Day, I’m once again recommitting to ensuring that our military members returning from the battlefield receive the benefits they earned. That means fighting for their healthcare, adequate housing, and mental health services.

As a combat veteran, I’ll never stop speaking up for our heroes and heroines. Please join me in honoring those who gave their lives for our country.

Happy Memorial Day.

- Mike Thompson

body:
On this Memorial Day in honor of the heroes who made the ultimate sacrifice to our country, I hope you’ll join me in recommitting to this fight. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85725204/475681207/41388607?refcode=AU_AHC_FR_GEN_NOA_AC_20240527_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiM2RhYmJiOTktNGExYy1lZjExLTg2ZDAtNjA0NWJkZDllMDk2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=mH5dKDrrhXnOblTxjE5j-RaoOUQc_821OdKMusv9NN0=)
---
Peter — On September 11, 2001, I was fast asleep when my dad called me at 6:30 am. Planes had flown into the World Trade Center — America was under attack. I experienced the day the way most people did: watching TV in stunned silence. The world had changed before our very eyes. As I processed the shock and horror of watching the Twin Towers fall and smoke pour out of the Pentagon, I felt a profound call to serve and protect the ideals of the nation I’d come to call home. So I made the decision to enlist to serve in the military. When I raised my hand to take the oath of service, I made a commitment to protect America and our democracy from all enemies — foreign and domestic. That’s the same oath I will make as a member of Congress. And one I will never yield on. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85725205/475681208/695535462?refcode=AU_AHC_FR_GEN_NOA_AC_20240527_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiM2RhYmJiOTktNGExYy1lZjExLTg2ZDAtNjA0NWJkZDllMDk2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=mH5dKDrrhXnOblTxjE5j-RaoOUQc_821OdKMusv9NN0=) During my time as a U.S. Navy Reserve Intelligence Officer, I witnessed first-hand the dedication and determination of my fellow service members. **I served alongside incredible men and women who put their lives on the line. They asked for nothing in return, and many made the ultimate sacrifice in defense of our nation.** When David Schweikert voted against the _PACT Act_ — a bipartisan bill to provide health benefits to veterans who were exposed to toxic substances during their service — I was furious. Even Matt Gaetz and Ted Cruz voted yes. I was mad again, though maybe not surprised, when he tried to take credit for it. But I am turning that anger into action and running for Congress to help keep our promise to the families of every soldier we honor this Memorial Day. It’s also why I was outraged to hear earlier this month that the VA improperly gave out over $11 million in bonuses to their executives. Our fight to hold them accountable and ensure our veterans get the support they deserve couldn’t be more urgent. So on this Memorial Day in honor of the heroes who made the ultimate sacrifice to our country, I hope you’ll join me in recommitting to this fight. Just as my decision to enlist was a call to service, my campaign for Congress is a call to action. Together, we can build a better future for our veterans and for our nation. In your service, Andrei | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85725207/475681213/695535462?refcode=AU_AHC_FR_GEN_NOA_AC_20240527_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDUwMDAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiM2RhYmJiOTktNGExYy1lZjExLTg2ZDAtNjA0NWJkZDllMDk2IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=mH5dKDrrhXnOblTxjE5j-RaoOUQc_821OdKMusv9NN0=)

name: Lanon Baccam
subject: What Memorial Day means to a veteran:
body:
www.lanonbaccam.com [www.lanonbaccam.com]
Dear Peter,

Twenty years ago this month, I landed in Kandahar, Afghanistan with my Iowa Army National Guard unit. We were the first full time group to operate out of the Kandahar Provincial Reconstruction Team (PRT) base located just inside the city - about 45 minutes from the main U.S. air base.

Our deployment lasted a year in the stifling hot and extremely arid desert plateau around Kandahar. There were only a few dozen of us on the PRT to take care of everything from providing force protection for our missions to vehicle maintenance to base construction and building out fortifications.

The days were long and dusty with dull brown and gray desert as far as the eye could see. As a combat engineer I spent my time building everything from chairs, desks, and tables, to small buildings, bunkers, and observation towers. I also planned explosive demolitions every couple weeks for the unexploded ordnance, weapons, and drugs we’d collect and confiscate on our missions.

We all had our jobs to do - from the infantry, cooks, medics, mechanics, engineers, and others - and together, we became a team that built a brotherhood to defend each other no matter who you were or where you came from. This experience showed me the best values held by our country are clearly exemplified through selfless service.

We are stronger when we look out for each other rather than dividing ourselves along our perceived differences. Unfortunately, this mentality is no longer the default in our current state of politics.

It’s more important than ever before to restore our collective values as a country focused on community, defending our democracy, and protecting our freedoms.

There is no better way to gain perspective on this mission than to remember the individuals who have given the ultimate sacrifice in service to our country. On this Memorial Day, please take time to read their stories, and learn about the impact they had on their communities and how loved they were by their family and friends.

These stories always give me inspiration and remind me that those who choose to serve in the military don’t do so for a political party - we do it for the greater good because we know service to our country makes us part of something bigger than ourselves.

Today and every day, let’s honor the sacrifice so many have made for our country by committing ourselves to the pursuit of a more perfect union.

Thank you,

Lanon Baccam
www.lanonbaccam.com [www.lanonbaccam.com]
Lanon's campaign to flip Iowa's Third Congressional District and take back the House is fueled by grassroots supporters like you, Peter. We want to make sure you're getting our latest updates, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can click here to unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/s5InDMXi8EC0qnjjUXh5Pg2?unsubscribedata=t9WLotlVPxUJNE944MN1Lflfl5J5bWPKLfbD4fkcq2hfFfAlGFUbziDUAC59oPmiwhv7sybGDSHx%2FvxXP3mTqHMwh1m8XwYABn401hcEp%2B%2BDYDE7bouJXg7LdoLvTkv%2BzF9XwTiuQ0EdbIz%2FGfscvvV07lRADKnzvi4KLEsJ1EPxQibaWNhTgfDeVfqZ%2FvWN7VP0olFlxmvp1DpYUc0XZ%2FjX0JUxWDB%2BWzKKk%2Bw9zUvMucu9MyeDc4jsFCaKsPp083RnSCvyQJmcD7T8QlM6WiXDASh196JHct8ztEbokarkvXKNAWJjKnGCuxO7s3zuVqm6V8B91n9OSrJq2ymU3TQJp1E%2BKf5f5FSrP%2BVc2naFsE0Zob220a%2BXM2eV9D8ZwSapZq44hviBlvEmMXg%2B4bTE8r9gUtfk99DH8qrUlHkh2l4XjrTMtwfRTxOcBV%2BN .
Baccam for Iowa
PO Box 930
Des Moines, IA 50304
United States
Lanon Baccam is a former member of the Iowa National Guard. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Iowa National Guard or the Department of Defense.

name: David Hogg
subject: It’s rare I get to sleep in my own bed anymore
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
I’ve been on the road helping our candidates so often lately that it’s rare I get to sleep in my own bed anymore.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20240527&t=1&akid=731%2E587540%2ExIrAYq)
Peter,
I’ve been on the road helping our candidates so often lately that it’s rare I get to sleep in my own bed anymore. But each time I get the news that someone we endorsed won their election, I know it’s worth it.
Here’s a few of the places I’ve been lately:
* **Orlando, FL to support Nate Douglas.** At 23 years old, Nate would be the youngest person in the Florida legislature.

* **Atlanta, GA to support Bryce Berry.** He’s 22 years old and would be the first public school teacher ever elected to the state legislature.

* **Houston, TX to support Molly Cook.** She’s also 23 years old and is currently the youngest and most progressive member of the Texas Senate.

[**This work is tiring, but it’s worth it to elect so many awesome young leaders — and none of it is possible without your support. Can you pitch in $5 today to help us keep electing young leaders across the country?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20240527?refcode=em20240527&amount=5&refcode2=731_587540_xIrAYq&akid=731%2E587540%2ExIrAYq)
**One thing I’ve learned is that not everyone does it like us.** A lot of big national organizations will parachute in, shell out thousands of dollars for really generic-seeming ads, and call it a day.
But being on the ground, meeting campaign staff, and talking directly to voters allows us to invest your donations much more effectively because we know exactly the best place to spend it.
We can identify what each campaign needs to move the needle in their races — whether that’s more doors knocked, a TV ad, a mail piece on a certain issue, or something else entirely.
**And the work is paying off, too**. Last year we elected our first endorsed candidate, **Nadarius Clark** , in Virginia. And just a couple weeks ago, **Molly Cook** won a special election to serve out the remainder of the current term in the Texas Senate (though she’ll still have to win the primary runoff and a full term in November).
We’re looking forward to electing Nate, Bryce, Molly, and all of our endorsed candidates this November, but we truly cannot do it without your support.
As a grassroots organization, we don’t take corporate donations. We’re counting on your help to support each of the candidates we endorse.
[**So Peter, if you want to help us keep electing fantastic young leaders all over the country, please pitch in $5 to Leaders We Deserve today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20240527?refcode=em20240527&amount=5&refcode2=731_587540_xIrAYq&akid=731%2E587540%2ExIrAYq)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20240527?amount=5&refcode=em20240527-731&refcode2=731_587540_xIrAYq&akid=731%2E587540%2ExIrAYq)**
Thank you so much for all of your support,
David Hogg
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20240527&t=2&refcode2=731_587540_xIrAYq&akid=731%2E587540%2ExIrAYq)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=731%2E587540%2ExIrAYq)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=731%2E587540%2ExIrAYq)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)
PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE.COM, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE

We'll give credit where credit is due. Mitt Romney has been one of the few Republicans to criticize Donald Trump, and to support impeachment. **But he's 100% wrong on this. Trump must pay for his crimes, and cannot be allowed to escape justice. And there is no evidence whatsoever that his lunatic followers would come to their senses if offered an olive branch in the form of a pardon.** Try him, convict him, and punish him -- but do NOT pardon him. name: Jimmy Panetta
subject: Happy Memorial Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jimmy Panetta for Congress 2024, FEC ID# C00592154
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85726496/475684106/-2113420158?source=EM_NBI_JP_20240527_ST_MemorialDay_0_3MO_JimmyPanetta_NA_NA_MemorialDay&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTkwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2M2EzY2NmZC00YjFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2yGFwF4GwfPJ-p-UHZKwAQ0Ukwpib7hvxrR2DWQuLOM=&emci=2f408e1b-7a17-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&emdi=63a3ccfd-4b1c-ef11-86d0-6045bdd9e096&ceid=6108787) | Peter,

**_Every Memorial Day,_** we formally honor the men and women of our Armed Services who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend freedom and uphold our values.

**_Every day as we live our lives,_** not only must we appreciate that sacrifice, but we must also be mindful and grateful for all who serve– and their families who support them. As a veteran of the Afghanistan War, a member of the House Armed Services Committee, a co-founder of the Congressional For Country Caucus, and a federal representative for veterans in California's 19th Congressional District, I will continue to fight for federal legislation that guarantees care for veterans and active-duty service members, lowers their housing costs, helps protect their lives, and provides support to their families.

Please join me in honoring the legacy of those who served and sacrificed on this Memorial Day and every day forward by continuing to stand up to ensure freedom for all Americans and to promote America's everlasting values.

– Jimmy Panetta

clean_body: | Peter, Every Memorial Day, we formally honor the men and women of our Armed Services who made the ultimate sacrifice to defend freedom and uphold our values. Every day as we live our lives, not only must we appreciate that sacrifice, but we must also be mindful and grateful for all who serve– and their families who support them. As a veteran of the Afghanistan War, a member of the House Armed Services Committee, a co-founder of the Congressional For Country Caucus, and a federal representative for veterans in California's 19th Congressional District, I will continue to fight for federal legislation that guarantees care for veterans and active-duty service members, lowers their housing costs, helps protect their lives, and provides support to their families. Please join me in honoring the legacy of those who served and sacrificed on this Memorial Day and every day forward by continuing to stand up to ensure freedom for all Americans and to promote America's everlasting values. – Jimmy Panetta If you'd like to receive only the most important updates from us, Authorized and Paid for by Jimmy Panetta for Congress 2024, FEC ID# C00592154 Jimmy Panetta for Congress PO Box 103 Carmel Valley, CA 93924 United States ** **

| | Today is Memorial Day, and I wanted to take a moment to reflect on the significance of this important day. Memorial Day gives us an opportunity to pay tribute to the brave men and women who made the ultimate sacrifice in service to our country and who selflessly gave their lives defending the freedoms and values we hold dear. Each year, as we gather with family and friends, let us not forget the reason for this holiday. Let us take a moment to pause and reflect on the tremendous sacrifices made by our servicemembers and their families. Their courage, dedication, and unwavering commitment to duty are what make our nation strong and free. As we enjoy the freedoms and opportunities afforded to us as Americans, let us honor the memory of our fallen heroes, and strive to live lives worthy of their sacrifice, upholding the principles of freedom, justice, and equality for all. This Memorial Day, let us all join together in honoring and remembering the brave men and women who have given everything for our country. More soon, _Ron_ |
---|---|---

name: Sara Jacobs
subject: Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sara Jacobs for Congress
body:
Sara Jacobs - Democrat, U.S. Congress [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/89313/images/SDS/SJJ-emailheader1.png]
This Memorial Day, I’m thinking of the countless brave service members who have sacrificed everything to protect our values, our ideals, and our nation – and of the many military families who have lost loved ones.
I know the San Diego community deeply understands the sacrifice involved in service, so I hope you’ll join me in reflecting on the courage of all the service members who have given their lives in service to our country.
We owe a debt of gratitude that is impossible to repay.
But today and every day, we should strive to honor our fallen heroes by supporting all members of our military community and ensuring they can live with the dignity and respect they deserve.
Thanks for reading.
Sara

clean_body: Friend, I need you to listen: | | | = Friend, Florida is on the cusp of flipping blue this November — but I need your help to make sure we don’t waste this incredible opportunity. Please, will you let me explain? = You see, activists in Florida officially secured enough signatures to put abortion rights on the ballot this November. If Florida follows states like Kansas and Ohio, this ballot initiative will pass in a landslide, restoring reproductive rights and ending the GOP’s extreme ban. And it could drive up Democratic turnout across the board, giving Debbie a critical boost. But that’s not all: Democrats also won a key special election here, flipping a critical State House District from red to blue against all the odds! And, polling data shows that Debbie is polling 4 points ahead! Listen, a blue wave is building in Florida — and our party is preparing to make historic investments to help Debbie win this race. But we’re not the only ones investing in Florida: Rick Scott has spent over $4 MILLION airing attack ads. His unprecedented spending proves that he’s terrified to lose, but it also proves Debbie needs strong, sustained investments from grassroots Democrats to win. That’s why I’m reaching out to you. Debbie is up against an urgent $800,000 end-of-month goal, and she can’t hit it without a huge outpouring of grassroots support in the next few days. = If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = Thank you, Chuck Schumer | * | | | = Debbie Mucarsel-Powell is a Floridian, South American immigrant, mother, and former Congresswoman. Now, she’s running to defeat Republican Senator Rick Scott and win a better, brighter future for Florida.

name: Jena Griswold
subject: Honoring Our Heroes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://jenaforcolorado.com/ [https://jenaforcolorado.com/]
Happy Memorial Day! I want to take a moment to reflect on the significance of this important holiday. Today, we honor the brave men and women who have made the ultimate sacrifice to protect the freedoms we hold dear. Their courage and dedication have shaped the foundation of our great nation, and we owe them an immeasurable debt of gratitude.As we remember and pay tribute to these heroes, let us also cherish the time we have with our loved ones. Whether you are gathering with family and friends, enjoying a backyard barbecue, or exploring the stunning landscapes that Colorado has to offer, I hope you find peace and joy in these moments.Let us keep the spirit of Memorial Day in our hearts and strive to support our veterans, their families, and all those who serve our country.Wishing you all a safe, happy, and reflective Memorial Day.Jena Griswoldhttps://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/ [https://www.facebook.com/Jenaforcolorado/] https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold [https://twitter.com/JenaGriswold] https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold [https://www.instagram.com/jenagriswold]

name: Jill Stein for President
subject: Last day! Pull out all the stops for NY!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jill Stein for President 2024
body:
Jill urgently needs you now.

| | | [ ](https://www.jillstein2024.com/?e=e813143e1dc0d3b0ffcb007ffe357333&utm_source=jillsteinforpresident&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=5_27_ny_last_day&n=1)
---
Peter, One day left. That is all the time we have to get Jill on the ballot in New York and guarantee voters have an alternative to the parties of war and Wall Street across the country in November. Because every moment is precious for the campaign \- and for ending genocide abroad and rising fascism at home, for getting health care and housing as human rights and so much more. For all these reasons we are getting straight to the point with this final, urgent request. If you’re not in New York, please donate whatever you can today so that we can pay signature gatherers, travel and housing costs for volunteers, and to help Jill stay on track to get on every ballot across the country:
---
| [](https://www.jillstein2024.com/donation_ny?e=e813143e1dc0d3b0ffcb007ffe357333&utm_source=jillsteinforpresident&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=5_27_ny_last_day&n=2 "DONATE") [DONATE](https://www.jillstein2024.com/donation_ny?e=e813143e1dc0d3b0ffcb007ffe357333&utm_source=jillsteinforpresident&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=5_27_ny_last_day&n=2)
---
For all supporters**in New York City and Long Island (or anyone who can collect AND deliver to the Brooklyn office tonight) –** **we urgently need you to collect any signatures you can through tonight/Monday and bring them to the office at 31 Smith Street,** **2nd floor, Brooklyn NY 11201 by 8 PM!** (If you have signatures but can’t get them to the office by 8 PM tonight, please get them to us tomorrow/Tuesday morning and email [chair@gpny.org](mailto:chair@gpny.org) today to let us know!) If you are in the Albany area and can deliver your signatures tomorrow/Tuesday, **in Albany please keep collecting and stand by for instructions on where and when to deliver them in Albany!** We are now arranging for an Albany collection. To print petitions or to get more info, email [chair@gpny.org](mailto:chair@gpny.org), or go to the Green Party of New York’s website:
---
| [](https://www.jillstein2024.com/r?u=6F-3mfh77DKDOS_EiGgoH49D0ZLRfo3-LBulbZ6lbNsomRwOVHb0uZ2Dm_gr0Tz5&e=e813143e1dc0d3b0ffcb007ffe357333&utm_source=jillsteinforpresident&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=5_27_ny_last_day&n=3 "NY BALLOT INFO") [NY BALLOT INFO](https://www.jillstein2024.com/r?u=6F-3mfh77DKDOS_EiGgoH49D0ZLRfo3-LBulbZ6lbNsomRwOVHb0uZ2Dm_gr0Tz5&e=e813143e1dc0d3b0ffcb007ffe357333&utm_source=jillsteinforpresident&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=5_27_ny_last_day&n=3)
---
With all the Democrats’ dirty tricks and legal nitpicking, and RFK buying up nearly all professional petitioners, we all need to go the extra mile to be sure we get the only anti-genocide, pro-worker campaign on track to challenge empire across the country on the ballot in NY state! As Jill says, “If you believe in this movement for people, planet, and peace, I ask you to join me in this historic moment and do whatever you can to help us get on the ballot in New York NOW!” In Solidarity, -The Jill Stein 2024 team

subject: FWD: making sure you saw this, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James for NY 2026
body:
I’m sorry for reaching out again, Peter, but I needed to make sure you saw this.
---
Powerful and corrupt men just can’t seem to get out of the headlines, and they’re doing everything they can to take me down and silence me.
I said it once and I’ll say it again –******I will _never_ bow, I will _never_ break, and I will _never_ be bullied by the bad actors.** And I’ve only continued to uphold that vow in my fight for the vulnerable and voiceless.
**But my battle is far from over, Peter, and I’m going to need all the help I can get from fearless fighters like you to continue taking down these powerful men.[So I have to ask: Will you rush a donation of $5 or more now to help me stand tall against the attacks from the most powerful and corrupt?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/85728698/475688410/-2022193277?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk1462-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTg0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMThhMDgzMC01MDFjLWVmMTEtODZkMC02MDQ1YmRkOWUwOTYiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UVIywsA6fUETprRh-Ahx5izXngDA20c3M10bJMv-pik=)**

Letitia James

_It certainly doesn't shock me!_
**Crooked, corrupt politicians might try their hardest to get away with their crimes, but I’m determined to do everything in my power to finally bring shady forces to justice.**

Yes, I’ve gained new enemies since standing up against these powerful people and groups, but I’ve also helped and defended hardworking New Yorkers – and I wouldn’t have it any other way.
But my fight for the vulnerable and voiceless is far from over.

name: Sarah McBride
subject: Honoring those who gave their lives in service to our nation
body:
This Memorial Day, I hope everyone has the opportunity to take some time to remember those who have given their lives in service to our nation.
#
This Memorial Day, I hope everyone has the opportunity to take some time to remember those who have given their lives in service to our nation.
Last week, I was honored to march in the Newark Memorial Day Parade after joining Representatives Romer, Baumbach, and Osienski for a moving ceremony.
I’ll be participating in more events around Delaware today and throughout this coming week to honor those service members who gave the ultimate sacrifice to keep all of us safe. And if you have the chance, I hope you can participate, too.
With gratitude,
Sarah McBride

name: Glenn Grothman
subject: On this Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Glenn Grothman for Congress](https://links
body:
| | |
---
| | Happy Memorial Day, my friend. This weekend, we remember that "all gave some, some gave all" for the freedoms we cherish as Americans. It's easy to overlook the profound sacrifices our ancestors, friends, and family have made to safeguard our country and our way of life. Today, I hope you will take a moment to reflect on those we have lost in the fight for America, honoring their lives and the noble cause our nation's veterans upheld. In Congress, I will always honor their memory and remain steadfast in my commitment to upholding the cause of freedom. Thank you, and God Bless America. **Glenn Grothman**
---

| | I’ve got good news and bad news (_and who knows, maybe some extra good news_) for you, Peter: **Here’s the good news:** Thanks to our victory in the Maryland Democratic Primary, we’ve proven that with enough organizing, strategy, and outreach, we’re more than up to the task to win tough races — even when we’re being outspent 10-1. **But here’s the bad news:** This general election race is going to be an entirely different animal. We’re up against more powerful forces than ever, because with the Senate majority on the line, we’re taking on Larry Hogan + Mitch McConnell + dark money GOP super PACs. Maryland hasn’t seen a Senate race with higher stakes, so we need to be ready for anything and everything. We need to be nimble, responsive, and aggressive. **And here’s a little extra good news:[In a race this close, your support can and will be the difference!
